rows.english: From the suttas and from logic.

rows.pali: Sutte hi "cittānuparivattino dhammā"tiādinā (dha. sa. dukamātikā 62) nayena bahudhā vedanādīnaṃ viññāṇapaccayatā siddhā.
rows.english: For in the suttas it is established in many places that feeling, etc., have consciousness as condition in the way beginning, “States with parallel occurrence through consciousness” (Dhs §1522).

rows.pali: Yuttito pana,
rows.english: But as to logic:

rows.pali: Citte hi pasanne appasanne vā tadanurūpāni rūpāni uppajjamānāni diṭṭhāni.
rows.english: Whether consciousness likes it or not, [certain] material instances are seen to arise in conformity with it.

rows.pali: Diṭṭhena ca adiṭṭhassa anumānaṃ hotīti iminā idha diṭṭhena cittajarūpena adiṭṭhassāpi paṭisandhirūpassa viññāṇaṃ paccayo hotīti jānitabbametaṃ.
rows.english: And the unseen is inferred from the seen. So it can be known, by means of the consciousness-born materiality that is seen, that consciousness is also a condition for the unseen materiality of rebirth-linking.

rows.pali: Kammasamuṭṭhānassāpi hi tassa cittasamuṭṭhānasseva viññāṇapaccayatā paṭṭhāne āgatāti.
rows.english: For it is said in the Paṭṭhāna that, like the consciousness-originated, also the kamma-originated has consciousness as its condition (see Paṭṭh I 172–73).

rows.pali: Evaṃ paccayanayatopettha viññātabbo vinicchayoti.
rows.english: This is how the exposition should be known “by manner of condition. ”

rows.pali: 641.Nāmarūpapaccayā saḷāyatanapade –
rows.english: 203. As to the clause “With mentality-materiality as condition, the sixfold base”:

rows.pali: Yañhetaṃ saḷāyatanasseva paccayabhūtaṃ nāmarūpaṃ, tattha nāmanti vedanādikkhandhattayaṃ, rūpaṃ pana sasantatipariyāpannaṃ niyamato cattāri bhūtāni cha vatthūni jīvitindriyanti evaṃ bhūtavatthādikaṃ matanti veditabbaṃ.
rows.english: 204. In the case of the mentality-materiality that is here a condition for the sixfold base, mentality is the three aggregates beginning with feeling, while materiality should be understood as that included in one’s own continuity stated thus “primaries and the rest are ‘matter’,” that is to say, the four primaries, six physical bases, and life faculty, [since they are conditioning factors] invariably.

rows.pali: Taṃ pana nāmañca rūpañca nāmarūpañca nāmarūpanti evaṃ katekasesaṃ chaṭṭhāyatanañca saḷāyatanañca saḷāyatananti evaṃ katekasesasseva saḷāyatanassa paccayoti veditabbaṃ.
rows.english: But this mentality and this materiality and this mentality-materiality, each one representing the rest as “mentality-materiality,” should be understood as a condition for the sixfold base consisting of the sixth base and the sixfold base each one representing the rest as the “sixfold base.”

rows.pali: Kasmā?
rows.english: Why?

rows.pali: Yasmā āruppe nāmameva paccayo, tañca chaṭṭhāyatanasseva na aññassa.
rows.english: Because in the immaterial becoming there is only mentality as a condition, and that is a condition only for the sixth base, [namely, the mind base,] not for any other.

rows.pali: "Nāmapaccayā chaṭṭhāyatana"nti (vibha. 322) hi vibhaṅge vuttaṃ.
rows.english: For it is said in the Vibhaṅga, “With mentality as condition, the sixth base” (Vibh 179).

rows.pali: Tattha siyā – kathaṃ panetaṃ jānitabbaṃ "nāmarūpaṃ saḷāyatanassa paccayo"ti?
rows.english: 205. Here it may be asked: “But how is it to be known that mentality-materiality is a condition for the sixfold base?”

rows.pali: Nāmarūpabhāve bhāvato.
rows.english: Because the latter exists when mentality- materiality exists.

rows.pali: Tassa tassa hi nāmassa rūpassa ca bhāve taṃ taṃ āyatanaṃ hoti, na aññathā.
rows.english: For a given base exists when a given kind of mentality and materiality exists, not otherwise.

rows.pali: Sā panassa tabbhāvabhāvitā paccayanayasmiṃ yeva āvibhavissati.
rows.english: But the way in which the one comes to exist when the other does will be explained below in the section dealing with how it is a condition.

rows.pali: Tasmā,
rows.english: Therefore:

rows.pali: Tatrāyamatthadīpanā –
rows.english: 206. Herein what follows illustrates the meaning.

rows.pali: Kathaṃ?
Paṭisandhiyaṃ tāva avakaṃsato sahajātaaññamaññanissayasampayuttavipākaatthiavigatapaccayehi sattadhā nāmaṃ chaṭṭhāyatanassa paccayo hoti.
rows.english: In rebirth-linking, firstly, mentality is a condition in seven ways at the minimum, as conascence, mutuality, support, association, kamma-result, presence, and non-disappearance conditions, for the sixth base.

rows.pali: Kiñci panettha hetupaccayena, kiñci āhārapaccayenāti evaṃ aññathāpi paccayo hoti, tassa vasena ukkaṃsāvakaṃso veditabbo.
rows.english: Some mentality, however, is a condition, as root-cause condition [that is, greed, etc.,] and some as nutriment condition [that is, contact and mental volition]. So it is also a condition in other ways. It is by the [two latter] that the maximum and minimum should be understood.

rows.pali: Pavattepi vipākaṃ vuttanayeneva paccayo hoti, itaraṃ pana avakaṃsato vuttappakāresu paccayesu vipākapaccayavajjehi chahi paccayehi paccayo hoti.
rows.english: In the course of an existence, too, resultant mentality is a condition as already stated. But the other [non-resultant] kind is a condition in six ways at minimum, as the aforesaid conditions except for kamma-result condition.

rows.pali: Kiñci panettha hetupaccayena, kiñci āhārapaccayenāti evaṃ aññathāpi paccayo hoti, tassa vasena ukkaṃsāvakaṃso veditabbo.
rows.english: Some, however, are a condition, as root-cause condition, and some as nutriment condition. So it is also a condition in other ways. It is by these that the maximum and minimum should be understood.

rows.pali: Āruppato hi aññasmimpi pañcavokārabhave taṃ vipākanāmaṃ hadayavatthuno sahāyaṃ hutvā chaṭṭhassa manāyatanassa yathā āruppe vuttaṃ, tatheva avakaṃsato sattadhā paccayo hoti.
rows.english: 209. Besides the immaterial states, also in the five-constituent becoming that resultant mentality, in association with the heart-basis, is a condition in seven ways at the minimum for the sixth, the mind base, in the same way as was said with respect to the immaterial states.

rows.pali: Itaresaṃ pana taṃ pañcannaṃ cakkhāyatanādīnaṃ catumahābhūtasahāyaṃ hutvā sahajātanissayavipākavippayuttaatthiavigatavasena chahākārehi paccayo hoti.
rows.english: But in association with the four primary elements, it is a condition in six ways, as conascence, support, kamma-result, dissociation, presence, and non-disappearance conditions, for the other five beginning with the eye base.

rows.pali: Kiñci panettha hetupaccayena, kiñci āhārapaccayenāti evaṃ aññathāpi paccayo hoti, tassa vasena ukkaṃsāvakaṃso veditabbo.
rows.english: Some, however, are a condition as root-cause condition, and some as nutriment condition. It is by these that the maximum and minimum should be understood.

rows.pali: Pavattepi hi pañcavokārabhave yathā paṭisandhiyaṃ, tatheva vipākanāmaṃ vipākassa chaṭṭhāyatanassa avakaṃsato sattadhā paccayo hoti.
rows.english: 211. For, as in rebirth-linking, so also in the course of an existence in the five- constituent becoming, resultant mentality is a condition in the seven ways at minimum for the resultant sixth base.

rows.pali: Avipākaṃ pana avipākassa chaṭṭhassa avakaṃsatova tato vipākapaccayaṃ apanetvā chadhā paccayo hoti.
rows.english: But non-resultant mentality is a condition in six ways at minimum for the non-resultant sixth base, leaving out kamma- result condition.

rows.pali: Vuttanayeneva panettha ukkaṃsāvakaṃso veditabbo.
rows.english: The maximum and minimum should be understood in the way already stated.

rows.pali: Tattheva hi pavatte sesānaṃ cakkhāyatanādīnaṃ pañcannaṃ cakkhupasādādivatthukaṃ itarampi vipākanāmaṃ pacchājātavippayuttaatthiavigatapaccayehi catudhā paccayo hoti.
rows.english: 213. Again, in the course of an existence, the other resultant mentality, which has as its physical basis the eye sensitivity, etc., is a condition in four ways, as postnascence, dissociation, presence, and non-disappearance conditions, for the rest of the five beginning with the eye base.

rows.pali: Yathā ca vipākaṃ, avipākampi evameva pakāsitaṃ.
rows.english: And as the resultant, so also the non-resultant is explained;

rows.pali: Tasmā kusalādibhedampi tesaṃ catudhā paccayo hotīti veditabbaṃ.
rows.english: therefore [the mentality] classed as profitable, etc., should be understood as their condition in four ways.

rows.pali: Evaṃ tāva nāmameva paṭisandhiyaṃ pavatte vā yassa yassa āyatanassa paccayo hoti, yathā ca paccayo hoti, tathā veditabbaṃ.
rows.english: This, firstly, is how it should be understood what bases mentality alone is a condition for in rebirth-linking and in the course of an existence, and how it is a condition.

rows.pali: Rūpato hi paṭisandhiyaṃ vatthurūpaṃ chaṭṭhassa manāyatanassa sahajātaaññamaññanissayavippayuttaatthiavigatapaccayehi chadhā paccayo hoti.
rows.english: 215. As to matter, the materiality of the physical [heart-] basis is a condition in rebirth-linking in six ways, as conascence, mutuality, support, dissociation, presence, and non-disappearance conditions, for the sixth, the mind base.

rows.pali: Cattāri pana bhūtāni avisesato paṭisandhiyaṃ pavatte ca yaṃ yaṃ āyatanaṃ uppajjati, tassa tassa vasena pañcannampi cakkhāyatanādīnaṃ sahajātanissayaatthiavigatapaccayehi catudhā paccayā honti.
rows.english: But the four primaries are in general, that is to say, in rebirth-linking and in the course of an existence, conditions in four ways, as conascence, support, presence, and non-disappearance conditions, for any of the five bases beginning with the eye, whenever they arise.

rows.pali: Etesaṃ pana cakkhādīnaṃ pañcannaṃ paṭisandhiyaṃ pavatte ca atthi avigataindriyavasena rūpajīvitaṃ tidhā paccayo hoti.
rows.english: 217. But in rebirth-linking and in the course of an existence the material life [faculty] is a condition in three ways, as presence, non-disappearance, and faculty conditions, for these five beginning with the eye.

rows.pali: Āhāro ca atthiavigatāhāravasena tividhā paccayo hoti, so ca kho ye sattā āhārūpajīvino, tesaṃ āhārānugate kāye pavattiyaṃyeva, no paṭisandhiyaṃ.
rows.english: Nutriment too is a condition in three ways, as presence, non-disappearance, and nutriment conditions, and that is so in the course of an existence, not in rebirth-linking, and applies when the bodies of beings subsisting on nutriment are suffused with the nutriment.

rows.pali: Tāni pana pañca cakkhāyatanādīni chaṭṭhassa cakkhu sotaghānajivhākāyaviññāṇasaṅkhātassa manāyatanassa nissayapurejātaindriyavippayuttaatthiavigatavasena chahākārehi paccayā honti pavatte, no paṭisandhiyaṃ.
rows.english: In the course of an existence, not in rebirth-linking, those five bases beginning with the eye are conditions in six ways, as support, prenascence, faculty, dissociation, presence and non-disappearance conditions, for [that part of] the sixth, the mind base, comprising eye-, ear-, nose-, tongue-, and body-consciousness.

rows.pali: Ṭhapetvā pana pañcaviññāṇāni tasseva avasesamanāyatanassa vatthurūpaṃ nissayapurejātavippayuttaatthiavigatavasena pañcadhā paccayo hoti pavatteyeva, no paṭisandhiyaṃ.
rows.english: But in the course of an existence, not at rebirth-linking, the materiality of the [heart- ] basis is a condition in five ways, as support, prenascence, dissociation, presence, and non-disappearance conditions, for the remaining mind base apart from the five consciousnesses.

rows.pali: Evaṃ rūpameva paṭisandhiyaṃ pavatte vā yassa yassa āyatanassa paccayo hoti, yathā ca paccayo hoti, tathā veditabbaṃ.
rows.english: This is how it should be understood what bases materiality alone is a condition for in rebirth-linking and in the course of an existence, and how it is a condition.

rows.pali: Seyyathidaṃ.
rows.english: 219. For example,

rows.pali: Paṭisandhiyaṃ tāva pañcavokārabhave khandhattayavatthurūpasaṅkhātaṃ nāmarūpaṃ chaṭṭhāyatanassa sahajātaaññamaññanissayavipākasampayuttavippayuttaatthiavigatapaccayādīhi paccayo hotīti.
rows.english: firstly, in rebirth-linking in the five-constituent becoming, the mentality-materiality, in other words, the trio of aggregates with the materiality of the [heart-] basis, is a condition, as conascence, mutuality, support, kamma- result, association, dissociation, presence, and non-disappearance conditions, etc., for the sixth, the mind base.

rows.pali: Idamettha mukhamattaṃ.
rows.english: This is merely the heading;

rows.pali: Vuttanayānusārena pana sakkā sabbaṃ yojetunti na ettha vitthāro dassitoti.
rows.english: but since it can all be construed in the way already stated, the detail is not given here.

rows.pali: Ayaṃ "nāmarūpapaccayā saḷāyatana"nti padasmiṃ vitthārakathā.
rows.english: This is the detailed explanation of the clause “With mentality-materiality as condition, the sixfold base.

rows.pali: 642.Saḷāyatanapaccayā phassapade –
rows.english: 220. As to the clause “With the sixfold base as condition, contact”:

rows.pali: Saṅkhepena hi saḷāyatanapaccayā phassoti cakkhusamphasso, sotasamphasso, ghānasamphasso, jivhāsamphasso, kāyasamphasso, manosamphassoti ime cakkhusamphassādayo cha eva phassā bhavanti.
rows.english: 221. Briefly, with the clause “With the sixfold base as condition, contact,” there are only the six kinds beginning with eye-contact, that is to say, eye-contact, ear-contact, nose-contact, tongue-contact, body-contact, and mind-contact.

rows.pali: Vitthārena pana cakkhusamphassādayo pañca kusalavipākā, pañca akusalavipākāti dasa, sesā bāvīsati-lokiyavipākaviññāṇasampayuttā ca bāvīsatīti evaṃ sabbepi saṅkhārapaccayā vuttaviññāṇamiva bāttiṃsa honti.
rows.english: But in detail the five profitable resultant and the five unprofitable resultant beginning with eye-contact make ten; the rest, which are associated with the twenty-two kinds of mundane resultant consciousness, make twenty-two. So all these come to thirty-two ((34)–(65)), like the consciousness with formations as condition given above.

rows.pali: Yaṃ panetassa bāttiṃsavidhassāpi phassassa paccayo saḷāyatanaṃ, tattha,
rows.english: 222. But as to the sixfold base that is a condition for this thirty-twofold contact. Herein:

rows.pali: Tattha ye tāva "upādiṇṇakapavattikathā aya"nti sakasantatipariyāpannameva paccayaṃ paccayuppannañca dīpenti, te "chaṭṭhāyatanapaccayā phasso"ti (vibha. 322) pāḷianusārato āruppe chaṭṭhāyatanañca, aññattha sabbasaṅgahato saḷāyatanañca phassassa paccayoti ekadesasarūpekasesaṃ katvā chaṭṭhena saha ajjhattaṃ cakkhādiṃ saḷāyatananti icchanti.
rows.english: 223. Herein, firstly, there are those who take this to be an exposition of the occurrence of what is clung to, [that is, kammically-acquired aggregates,] and they maintain that the conditioning [bases] and the conditionally-arisen [contact] are only what is included in one’s own continuity. They take any one part to represent any remaining one of its kind, since the condition for contact in the immaterial states is the sixth base [only], according to the text “With the sixth base as condition, contact” (Vibh 179), and elsewhere it is the sixfold base inclusively. So they have it that “sixfold base” means the internal [five] beginning with the eye plus the sixth (mind) base.

rows.pali: Tañhi chaṭṭhāyatanañca saḷāyatanañca saḷāyatanantveva saṅkhaṃ gacchati.
rows.english: For that sixth base and that sixfold base are styled “sixfold base. ”

rows.pali: Ye pana paccayuppannameva ekasantatipariyāpannaṃ dīpenti, paccayaṃ pana bhinnasantānampi, te yaṃ yaṃ āyatanaṃ phassassa paccayo hoti, taṃ sabbampi dīpentā bāhirampi pariggahetvā tadeva chaṭṭhena saha ajjhattaṃ bāhirehipi rūpāyatanādīhi saddhiṃ saḷāyatananti icchanti.
rows.english: But there are those who maintain that it is only the conditionally-arisen [contact] that is contained in a single continuity, while the conditioning [bases] are contained in separate [that is, past] continuities as well. They maintain that all and any such bases are a condition for contact, and they include also the [six] external ones. So they have it that “sixfold base” means the same internal [five] plus the sixth plus the external ones beginning with visible data.

rows.pali: Tampi hi chaṭṭhāyatanañca saḷāyatanañca saḷāyatananti etesaṃ ekasese kate saḷāyatanantveva saṅkhaṃ gacchati.
rows.english: For that sixth base and that [partial] sixfold base and the sixfold base along with these [external ones] each representing the rest [566] are styled sixfold base too.

rows.pali: Etthāha – na sabbāyatanehi eko phasso sambhoti, nāpi ekamhā āyatanā sabbe phassā, ayañca saḷāyatanapaccayā phassoti ekova vutto, so kasmāti.
rows.english: 224. Here it may be asked: “One kind of contact does not derive from all the bases, nor all the kinds of contact from one base. And yet ‘With the sixfold base as condition, contact’ is said in the singular. Why is that? ”

rows.pali: Tatridaṃ vissajjanaṃ – saccametaṃ, sabbehi eko, ekamhā vā sabbe na sambhonti, sambhoti pana anekehi eko.
rows.english: 225. Here is the answer: It is true that neither is one derived from all nor all from one. However, one is derived from many.

rows.pali: Yathā cakkhusamphasso cakkhāyatanā rūpāyatanā cakkhuviññāṇasaṅkhātā manāyatanā avasesasampayuttadhammāyatanā cāti evaṃ sabbattha yathānurūpaṃ yojetabbaṃ.
rows.english: For eye-contact is derived from the eye base, from the visible-data base, from the mind base reckoned as eye- consciousness, and from the mental-datum base consisting of the remaining associated states. And each case should be construed as appropriate in this way.

rows.pali: Tasmā eva hi,
rows.english: Therefore:

rows.pali: Ekavacananiddesenāti saḷāyatanapaccayā phassoti iminā ekavacananiddesena anekehi āyatanehi eko phasso hotīti tādinā dīpitoti attho.
rows.english: Though stated in the singular: the meaning is, by this statement in the singular that “With the sixfold base as condition, contact,” it is pointed out by the Blessed One (Tādin) that contact, which is of one kind, comes into being from many bases.

rows.pali: Āyatanesu pana,
rows.english: 226. But as regards these bases:

rows.pali: Tatrāyaṃ vibhāvanā – cakkhāyatanādīni tāva pañca cakkhusamphassādibhedato pañcavidhassa phassassa nissayapurejātindriyavippayuttaatthiavigatavasena chadhā paccayā honti.
rows.english: 227. Here is the explanation: firstly, the five consisting of the eye base, etc., are conditions in six ways, as support, prenascence, faculty, dissociation, presence, and non-disappearance conditions, for contact classed in five ways as eye- contact, and so on.

rows.pali: Tato paraṃ ekaṃ vipākamanāyatanaṃ anekabhedassa vipākamanosamphassassa sahajātaaññamaññanissayavipākāhāraindriyasampayuttaatthiavigatavasena navadhā paccayo hoti.
rows.english: After that, the single resultant mind base is a condition in nine ways, as conascence, mutuality, support, result, nutriment, faculty, association, presence, and non-disappearance conditions, for the variously- classed resultant mind contact.

rows.pali: Bāhiresu pana rūpāyatanaṃ cakkhusamphassassa ārammaṇapurejātaatthiavigatavasena catudhā paccayo hoti.
rows.english: But in the case of the external bases, the visible- data base is a condition in four ways, as object, prenascence, presence, and non- disappearance conditions, for eye-contact.

rows.pali: Tathā saddāyatanādīni sotasamphassādīnaṃ.
rows.english: Likewise the sound base, etc., respectively for ear-contact, and so on.

rows.pali: Manosamphassassa pana tāni ca dhammāyatanañca tathā ca ārammaṇapaccayamatteneva cāti evaṃ bāhirāni cha yathāsambhavametassa paccayatte vibhāvayeti.
rows.english: But these and mental data as object are conditions likewise, and as object condition too, for mind-contact, so “the external six as contact’s conditionality according to each case we fix. ”

rows.pali: 643.Phassapaccayā vedanāpade –
rows.english: 228. As to the clause “With contact as condition, feeling”:

rows.pali: Etassapi padassa vibhaṅge "cakkhusamphassajā vedanā.
rows.english: 229. In the analysis of this clause [in the Vibhaṅga] only six kinds of feeling according to door are given thus, “Eye-contact-born feeling,

rows.pali: Sota… ghāna… jivhā… kāya… manosamphassajā vedanā"ti (vibha. 231) evaṃ dvārato saḷeva vedanā vuttā, tā pana pabhedena ekūnanavutiyā cittehi sampayuttattā ekūnanavuti matā.
rows.english: ear-, nose-, tongue-, body-, mind-contact-born feeling” (Vibh 136). Still, when classed according to association with the eighty-nine kinds of consciousness, they are “at nine and eighty sorts assessed. ”

rows.pali: Tattha hi pañcadvāre cakkhupasādādivatthukānaṃ pañcannaṃ vedanānaṃ cakkhusamphassādiko phasso sahajātaaññamaññanissayavipākaāhārasampayuttaatthiavigatavasena aṭṭhadhā paccayo hoti.
rows.english: 231. Herein, in the five doors contact beginning with eye-contact is a condition in eight ways, as conascence, mutuality, support, result, nutriment, association, presence, and non-disappearance conditions, for the five kinds of feeling that have respectively eye sensitivity, etc., as their physical basis.

rows.pali: Sesānaṃ pana ekekasmiṃ dvāre sampaṭicchanasantīraṇatadārammaṇavasena pavattānaṃ kāmāvacaravipākavedanānaṃ so cakkhusamphassādiko phasso upanissayavasena ekadhāva paccayo hoti.
rows.english: But that contact beginning with eye-contact is a condition in one way only, as decisive-support condition, for the rest of resultant feeling in the sense sphere occurring in each door as receiving, investigation and registration.

rows.pali: Manodvārepi so tathāti manodvārepi hi tadārammaṇavasena pavattānaṃ kāmāvacaravipākavedanānaṃ so sahajātamanosamphassasaṅkhāto phasso tatheva aṭṭhadhā paccayo hoti, paṭisandhibhavaṅgacutivasena pavattānaṃ tebhūmakavipākavedanānampi.
rows.english: 232. In the mind door in the same way: the contact called conascent mind-contact is also a condition in the same eight ways for sense-sphere resultant feeling occurring as registration in the mind door, and so also for the kinds of resultant feeling in the three planes occurring with rebirth-linking, life-continuum and death.

rows.pali: Yā pana tā manodvāre tadārammaṇavasena pavattā kāmāvacaravedanā, tāsaṃ manodvārāvajjanasampayutto manosamphasso upanissayavasena ekadhāva paccayo hotīti.
rows.english: But the mind-contact associated with mind-door adverting is a condition in one way only, as decisive-support condition, for the kinds of feeling that occur in the mind door as registration in the sense sphere.

rows.pali: 644.Vedanāpaccayā taṇhāpade –
rows.english: 233. As regards the clause “With feeling as condition, craving”:

rows.pali: Imasmiṃ hi pade seṭṭhiputto brāhmaṇaputtoti pitito nāmavasena putto viya "rūpataṇhā.
rows.english: 234. called after their objects, as a son is called after his father “banker’s son,” “brahman’s son" as “visible-data craving,

rows.pali: Sadda… gandha… rasa… phoṭṭhabba… dhammataṇhā"ti (vibha. 232) ārammaṇato nāmavasena vibhaṅge cha taṇhā dīpitā.
rows.english: sound, odour, flavour, tangible-data, and mental-data craving” (Vibh 136), Six kinds of craving are shown in the analysis of this clause [in the Vibhaṅga].

rows.pali: Tāsu pana taṇhāsu ekekā taṇhā pavattiākārato kāmataṇhā, bhavataṇhā, vibhavataṇhāti evaṃ tividhā matā.
rows.english: ” Each of these six kinds of craving is reckoned threefold according to its mode of occurrence as craving for sense desires, craving for becoming, or craving for non-becoming.

rows.pali: Rūpataṇhāyeva hi yadā cakkhussa āpāthamāgataṃ rūpārammaṇaṃ kāmassādavasena assādayamānā pavattati, tadā kāmataṇhā nāma hoti.
rows.english: 235. When visible-data craving occurs enjoying with sense-desire enjoyment a visible datum as object that has come into the focus of the eye, it is called craving for sense desires.

rows.pali: Yadā tadevārammaṇaṃ "dhuvaṃ sassata"nti pavattāya sassatadiṭṭhiyā saddhiṃ pavattati, tadā bhavataṇhā nāma hoti.
rows.english: But when [that same visible-data craving] occurs along with the eternity view that assumes that same object to be lasting and eternal, [568] it is called craving for becoming;

rows.pali: Sassatadiṭṭhisahagato hi rāgo bhavataṇhāti vuccati.
rows.english: for it is the greed accompanying the eternity view that is called craving for becoming.

rows.pali: Yadā pana tadevārammaṇaṃ "ucchijjati vinassatī"ti pavattāya ucchedadiṭṭhiyā saddhiṃ pavattati, tadā vibhavataṇhā nāma hoti.
rows.english: When it occurs along with the annihilation view that assumes that same object to break up and be destroyed, it is called craving for non-becoming;

rows.pali: Ucchedadiṭṭhisahagato hi rāgo vibhavataṇhāti vuccati.
rows.english: for it is the greed accompanying the annihilation view that is called craving for non-becoming.

rows.pali: Esa nayo saddataṇhādīsupīti.
rows.english: So also in the case of craving for sounds, and so on.

rows.pali: Etā aṭṭhārasa taṇhā honti.
rows.english: These amount to eighteen kinds of craving.

rows.pali: Tā ajjhattarūpādīsu aṭṭhārasa, bahiddhā aṭṭhārasāti chattiṃsa.
rows.english: The eighteen with respect to one’s own visible data (one’s own appearance), etc., and eighteen with respect to external [visible data (another’s appearance), etc.,] together make thirty-six kinds.

rows.pali: Iti atītā chattiṃsa, anāgatā chattiṃsa, paccuppannā chattiṃsāti aṭṭhasataṃ taṇhā honti.
rows.english: Thirty-six in the past, thirty-six in the future, and thirty-six in the present, make one-hundred-and-eight kinds of craving.

rows.pali: Tā puna saṅkheppamāṇā rūpādiārammaṇavasena cha, kāmataṇhādivasena vā tissova taṇhā hontīti veditabbā.
rows.english: When these are reduced again, they should be understood to amount to the six kinds only with visible data, etc., as object; and these, to three only, as craving for sense desires, and so on.

rows.pali: Yasmā panime sattā puttaṃ assādetvā putte mamattena dhātiyā viya rūpādiārammaṇavasena uppajjamānaṃ vedanaṃ assādetvā vedanāya mamattena rūpādiārammaṇadāyakānaṃ cittakāra-gandhabba-gandhika-sūda-tantavāyarasāyanavidhāyakavejjādīnaṃ mahāsakkāraṃ karonti.
rows.english: 236. Out of selfish affection for feeling after taking pleasure in it when it arises through a visible datum as object, etc., these beings accord much honour to painters, musicians, perfumers, cooks, weavers, distillers of elixirs,39 physicians, etc., who furnish respectively visible data as object, etc., just as out of affection for a child they reward the child’s nurse after taking pleasure in the child.

rows.pali: Tasmā sabbāpesā vedanāpaccayā taṇhā hotīti veditabbā.
rows.english: That is why it should be understood that these three kinds of craving have feeling as their condition.

rows.pali: Ekadhāti upanissayapaccayeneva paccayo hoti.
rows.english: In one way: it is a condition as decisive-support condition only.

rows.pali: Yasmā vā,
rows.english: 238. Or alternatively:

rows.pali: 645.Taṇhāpaccayā upādānapade –
rows.english: 239. As regards the clause “With craving as condition, clinging”:

rows.pali: Tatrāyaṃ vibhāvanā – kāmupādānaṃ, diṭṭhupādānaṃ, sīlabbatupādānaṃ, attavādupādānanti imāni tāvettha cattāri upādānāni.
rows.english: 240. Herein, this is the explanation: firstly, there are these four kinds of clinging here, namely, sense-desire clinging, [false-] view clinging, rules-and-vows clinging, and self-doctrine clinging.

rows.pali: Tesaṃ ayaṃ atthavibhāgo – vatthusaṅkhātaṃ kāmaṃ upādiyatīti kāmupādānaṃ, kāmo ca so upādānañcātipi kāmupādānaṃ.
rows.english: 241. 1. The analysis of meaning is this: it clings to the kind of sense-desire called sense-desire’s physical object (see Ch. IV, n. 24), thus it is sense-desire clinging. Also, it is sense-desire and it is clinging, thus it is sense-desire clinging.

rows.pali: Upādānanti daḷhaggahaṇaṃ.
rows.english: Clinging (upādāna) is firm grasping;

rows.pali: Daḷhattho hettha upasaddo upāyāsaupakaṭṭhādīsu viya.
rows.english: for here the prefix upa has the sense of firmness, as in upāyāsa (great misery—see §48) and upakuṭṭha (great pox),41 and so on.

rows.pali: Tathā diṭṭhi ca sā upādānañcāti diṭṭhupādānaṃ.
rows.english: Likewise, it is [false] view and it is clinging, thus it is [false-] view clinging;

rows.pali: Diṭṭhiṃ upādiyatīti vā diṭṭhupādānaṃ.
rows.english: or, it clings to [false] view, thus it is [false-] view clinging;

rows.pali: "Sassato attā ca loko cā"tiādīsu (dī. ni. 1.31) hi purimadiṭṭhiṃ uttaradiṭṭhi upādiyati.
rows.english: for in [the case of the false view] “The world and self are eternal” (D I 14), etc., it is the latter kind of view that clings to the former.

rows.pali: Tathā sīlabbataṃ upādiyatīti sīlabbatupādānaṃ.
rows.english: Likewise, it clings to rite and ritual, thus it is rules-and- vows clinging;

rows.pali: Sīlabbatañca taṃ upādānañcātipi sīlabbatupādānaṃ.
rows.english: also, it is rite and ritual and it is clinging, thus it is rules-and- vows clinging;

rows.pali: Gosīlagovatādīni hi "evaṃ suddhī"ti abhinivesato sayameva upādānāni.
rows.english: for ox asceticism, ox vows, etc. (see M I 387f.), are themselves kinds of clinging, too, because of the misinterpretation (insistence) that purification comes about in this way.

rows.pali: Tathā vadanti etenāti vādo.
rows.english: Likewise, they indoctrinate by means of that, thus that is doctrine;

rows.pali: Upādiyanti etenāti upādānaṃ.
rows.english: they cling by means of that, thus that is clinging.

rows.pali: Kiṃ vadanti, upādiyanti vā?
rows.english: What do they indoctrinate with? What do they cling to?

rows.pali: Attānaṃ.
rows.english: Self.

rows.pali: Attano vādupādānaṃ attavādupādānaṃ.
rows.english: The clinging to doctrines of self is self-doctrine clinging.

rows.pali: Attavādamattameva vā attāti upādiyanti etenāti attavādupādānaṃ.
rows.english: Or by means of that they cling to a self that is a mere doctrine of self; thus that is self-doctrine clinging.

rows.pali: Ayaṃ tāva tesaṃ atthavibhāgo.
rows.english: This, firstly, is the “analysis of meaning. ”

rows.pali: Dhammasaṅkhepavitthāre pana kāmupādānaṃ tāva "tattha katamaṃ kāmupādānaṃ?
rows.english: 242. 2. But as regards the brief and full account of states, firstly, “Herein, what is sense-desire clinging?

rows.pali: Yo kāmesu kāmacchando kāmarāgo kāmanandī kāmataṇhā kāmasneho kāmapariḷāho kāmamucchā kāmajjhosānaṃ, idaṃ vuccati kāmupādāna"nti (dha. sa. 1220; vibha. 938) āgatattā saṅkhepato taṇhādaḷhattaṃ vuccati.
rows.english: That which in the case of sense desires is lust for sense desires, greed for sense desires, delight in sense desires, craving for sense desires, fever of sense desires, infatuation with sense desires, committal to sense desires: that is called sense-desire clinging” (Dhs §1214). - in brief sense- desire clinging is called “firmness of craving”.

rows.pali: Taṇhādaḷhattaṃ nāma purimataṇhāupanissayapaccayena daḷhasambhūtā uttarataṇhāva.
rows.english: “Firmness of craving” is a name for the subsequent craving itself, which has become firm by the influence of previous craving, which acts as its decisive-support condition.

rows.pali: Keci panāhu "appattavisayapatthanā taṇhā andhakāre corassa hatthappasāraṇaṃ viya, sampattavisayaggahaṇaṃ upādānaṃ tasseva bhaṇḍaggahaṇaṃ viya.
rows.english: But some have said: Craving is the aspiring to an object that one has not yet reached, like a thief’s stretching out his hand in the dark; clinging is the grasping of an object that one has reached, like the thief’s grasping his objective.

rows.pali: Appicchatāsantuṭṭhitāpaṭipakkhā ca te dhammā.
rows.english: These states oppose fewness of wishes and contentment

rows.pali: Tathā pariyesanārakkhadukkhamūlā"ti.
rows.english: and so they are the roots of the suffering due to seeking and guarding (see D II 58f.).

rows.pali: Sesupādānattayaṃ pana saṅkhepato diṭṭhimattameva.
rows.english: The remaining three kinds of clinging are in brief simply [false] view.

rows.pali: Vitthārato pana pubbe rūpādīsu vuttaaṭṭhasatappabhedāyapi taṇhāya daḷhabhāvo kāmupādānaṃ.
rows.english: 243. In detail, however, sense-desire clinging is the firm state of the craving described above as of one-hundred-and-eight kinds with respect to visible data and so on.

rows.pali: Dasavatthukā micchādiṭṭhi diṭṭhupādānaṃ.
rows.english: [False-] view clinging is the ten-based wrong view,

rows.pali: Yathāha – "tattha katamaṃ diṭṭhupādānaṃ?
rows.english: according as it is said: “Herein what is [false-] view clinging?

rows.pali: Natthi dinnaṃ, natthi yiṭṭhaṃ - pe - sacchikatvā pavedentīti yā evarūpā diṭṭhi - pe - vipariyesaggāho.
rows.english: There is no giving, no offering, … [no good and virtuous ascetics and brahmans who have themselves] realized by direct-knowledge and declare this world and the other world: such view as this … such perverse assumption

rows.pali: Idaṃ vuccati diṭṭhupādāna"nti (dha. sa. 1221; vibha. 938).
rows.english: is called [false-]view clinging” (Vibh 375; Dhs §1215).

rows.pali: Sīlabbatehi suddhīti parāmasanaṃ pana sīlabbatupādānaṃ.
rows.english: Rules-and-vows clinging is the adherence [to the view that] purification comes through rules and vows,

rows.pali: Yathāha – "tattha katamaṃ sīlabbatupādānaṃ?
rows.english: according as it is said: “Herein, what is rules- and-vows clinging?…

rows.pali: Sīlena suddhi, vatena suddhi, sīlabbatena suddhīti yā evarūpā diṭṭhi - pe - vipariyesaggāho.
rows.english: That purification comes through a rite, that purification comes through a ritual, [570] that purification comes through a rite and ritual: such view as this … such perverse assumption is

rows.pali: Idaṃ vuccati sīlabbatupādāna"nti (dha. sa. 1222; vibha. 938).
rows.english: called rules-and-vows clinging” (Dhs §1216).

rows.pali: Vīsativatthukā sakkāyadiṭṭhi attavādupādānaṃ.
rows.english: Self-doctrine clinging is the twenty-based [false] view of individuality,

rows.pali: Yathāha – "tattha katamaṃ attavādupādānaṃ?
rows.english: according as it is said: “Herein, what is self-doctrine clinging?

rows.pali: Idha assutavā puthujjano - pe - sappurisadhamme avinīto rūpaṃ attato samanupassati - pe - vipariyesaggāho, idaṃ vuccati attavādupādāna"nti (dha. sa. 1223; vibha. 938).
rows.english: Here the untaught ordinary man … untrained in good men’s Dhamma, sees materiality as self … such perverse assumption is called self-doctrine clinging” (Dhs §1217).

rows.pali: Ayamettha dhammasaṅkhepavitthāro.
rows.english: This is the “brief and full account of states.”

rows.pali: Kamatoti ettha pana tividho kamo uppattikkamo pahānakkamo desanākkamo ca.
rows.english: 244. 3. As to order: here order is threefold (see XIV.211), that is to say, order of arising, order of abandoning, and order of teaching.

rows.pali: Tattha anamatagge saṃsāre imassa paṭhamaṃ uppattīti abhāvato kilesānaṃ nippariyāyena uppattikkamo na vuccati.
rows.english: Herein, order of arising of defilements is not meant literally because there is no first arising of defilements in the beginningless round of rebirths.

rows.pali: Pariyāyena pana yebhuyyena ekasmiṃ bhave attaggāhapubbaṅgamo sassatucchedābhiniveso, tato "sassato ayaṃ attā"ti gaṇhato attavisuddhatthaṃ sīlabbatupādānaṃ, "ucchijjatī"ti gaṇhato paralokanirapekkhassa kāmupādānanti evaṃ paṭhamaṃ attavādupādānaṃ, tato diṭṭhisīlabbatakāmupādānānīti ayametesaṃ ekasmiṃ bhave uppattikkamo.
rows.english: But in a relative sense it is this: usually in a single becoming the misinterpretation of (insistence on) eternity and annihilation are preceded by the assumption of a self. After that, when a man assumes that this self is eternal, rules-and-vows clinging arises in him for the purpose of purifying the self. And when a man assumes that it breaks up, thus disregarding the next world, sense-desire clinging arises in him. So self-doctrine clinging arises first, and after that, [false-] view clinging, and rules-and-vows clinging or sense-desire clinging. This, then, is their order of arising in one becoming.

rows.pali: Diṭṭhupādānādīni cettha paṭhamaṃ pahīyanti sotāpattimaggavajjhattā.
rows.english: 245. And here [false-] view clinging, etc., are abandoned first because they are eliminated by the path of stream-entry.

rows.pali: Kāmupādānaṃ pacchā, arahattamaggavajjhattāti ayametesaṃ pahānakkamo.
rows.english: Sense-desire clinging is abandoned later because it is eliminated by the path of Arahantship. This is the order of their abandoning.

rows.pali: Mahāvisayattā pana pākaṭattā ca etesu kāmupādānaṃ paṭhamaṃ desitaṃ.
rows.english: 246. Sense-desire clinging, however, is taught first among them because of the breadth of its objective field and because of its obviousness.

rows.pali: Mahāvisayaṃ hi taṃ aṭṭhacittasampayogā, appavisayāni itarāni catucittasampayogā, yebhuyyena ca ālayarāmattā pajāya pākaṭaṃ kāmupādānaṃ, na itarāni.
rows.english: For it has a broad objective field because it is associated with eight kinds of consciousness ((22)–(29)). The others have a narrow objective field because they are associated with four kinds of consciousness ((22), (23), (26) and (27)). And usually it is sense-desire clinging that is obvious because of this generation’s love of attachment (see M I 167), not so the other kinds.

rows.pali: Kāmupādāna vā kāmānaṃ samadhigamatthaṃ kotūhalamaṅgalādibahulo hoti, sāssa diṭṭhīti tadanantaraṃ diṭṭhupādānaṃ, taṃ pabhijjamānaṃ sīlabbataattavādupādānavasena duvidhaṃ hoti.
rows.english: One possessed of sense-desire clinging is much given to display and ceremony (see M I 265) for the purpose of attaining sense desires. [False-] view clinging comes next to the [sense-desire clinging] since that [display and ceremony] is a [false-] view of his.42

rows.pali: Tasmiṃ dvaye gokiriyaṃ kukkurakiriyaṃ vā disvāpi veditabbato oḷārikanti sīlabbatupādānaṃ paṭhamaṃ desitaṃ.
rows.english: And that is then divided in two as rules-and-vows clinging and self-doctrine clinging. And of these two, rules-and-vows clinging is taught first, being gross, because it can be recognized on seeing [it in the forms of] ox practice and dog practice.

rows.pali: Sukhumattā ante attavādupādānanti ayametesaṃ desanākkamo.
rows.english: And self-doctrine clinging is taught last because of its subtlety. This is the “order of teaching. ”

rows.pali: Ettha ca evaṃ desite upādānacatukke purimassa kāmupādānassa kāmataṇhā upanissayavasena ekadhāva paccayo hoti, taṇhābhinanditesu visayesu uppattito.
rows.english: 248. As regards the four kinds of clinging taught in this way, craving for sense desires is a condition in one way, as decisive-support, for the first kind, namely, sense-desire clinging, because it arises in relation to the objective field in which craving delights.

rows.pali: Sesattayassa pana sahajātaaññamaññanissayasampayuttaatthiavigatahetuvasena sattadhā vā, upanissayena saha aṭṭhadhā vāpi paccayo hoti.
rows.english: But it is a condition in seven ways, as conascence, mutuality, support, association, presence, non-disappearance, and root-cause, or in eight ways, as [those and] decisive-support as well, for the remaining three kinds.

rows.pali: Yadā ca sā upanissayavasena paccayo hoti, tadā asahajātāva hotīti.
rows.english: And when it is a condition as decisive-support, then it is never conascent.

rows.pali: 646.Upādānapaccayā bhavapade –
rows.english: 249. As to the clause “With clinging as condition, becoming”:

rows.pali: Tattha bhavatīti bhavo.
rows.english: 250. 1. As to meaning: Herein, it becomes (bhavati), thus it is becoming (bhava).

rows.pali: So kammabhavo upapattibhavo cāti duvidho hoti.
rows.english: That is twofold as kamma-process becoming and rebirth-process becoming,

rows.pali: Yathāha – "bhavo duvidhena atthi kammabhavo, atthi upapattibhavo"ti (vibha. 234).
rows.english: according as it is said: “Becoming in two ways: there is kamma-process becoming and there is rebirth-process becoming” (Vibh 137).

rows.pali: Tattha kammameva bhavo kammabhavo, tathā upapattiyeva bhavo upapattibhavo.
rows.english: Herein, the kamma process itself as becoming is “kamma-process becoming”; likewise the rebirth process itself as becoming is “rebirth-process becoming.”

rows.pali: Ettha ca upapatti bhavatīti bhavo.
rows.english: And here, rebirth is becoming since it becomes;

rows.pali: Kammaṃ pana yathā sukhakāraṇattā "sukho buddhānaṃ uppādo"ti (dha. pa. 194) vutto, evaṃ bhavakāraṇattā phalavohārena bhavoti veditabbanti.
rows.english: but just as “The arising of Buddhas is bliss” (Dhp 194) is said because it causes bliss, so too kamma should be understood as “becoming,” using for it the ordinary term for its fruit, since it causes becoming.

rows.pali: Evaṃ tāvettha atthato viññātabbo vinicchayo.
rows.english: This, firstly, is how the exposition should be known here “as to meaning.”

rows.pali: 647.Dhammato pana kammabhavo tāva saṅkhepato cetanā ceva cetanāsampayuttā ca abhijjhādayo kammasaṅkhātā dhammā.
rows.english: 251. 2. As to state: firstly, kamma-process becoming in brief is both volition and the states of covetousness, etc., associated with the volition and reckoned as kamma too,

rows.pali: Yathāha – "tattha katamo kammabhavo?
rows.english: according as it is said: “Herein, what is kamma-process becoming?

rows.pali: Puññābhisaṅkhāro apuññābhisaṅkhāro āneñjābhisaṅkhāro (vibha. 234) parittabhūmako vā mahābhūmako vā, ayaṃ vuccati kammabhavo.
rows.english: The formation of merit, the formation of demerit, the formation of the imperturbable, either with a small (limited) plane or with a large (exalted) plane: that is called kamma-process becoming.

rows.pali: Sabbampi bhavagāmikammaṃ kammabhavo"ti (vibha. 234).
rows.english: Also all kamma that leads to becoming is called kamma-process becoming” (Vibh 137).

rows.pali: Ettha hi puññābhisaṅkhāroti terasa cetanā.
rows.english: 252. Here the formation of merit is, in terms of states, the thirteen kinds of volition ((1)–(13)),

rows.pali: Apuññābhisaṅkhāroti dvādasa.
rows.english: the formation of demerit is the twelve kinds ((22)–(33)),

rows.pali: Āneñjābhisaṅkhāroti catasso cetanā.
rows.english: and the formation of the imperturbable is the four kinds ((14)–(17)).

rows.pali: Evaṃ parittabhūmako vā mahābhūmako vāti etena tāsaṃyeva cetanānaṃ mandabahuvipākatā vuttā.
rows.english: So with the words either with a small (limited) plane or with a large (exalted) plane the insignificance or magnitude of these same volitions’ result is expressed here.

rows.pali: Sabbampi bhavagāmikammanti iminā pana cetanāsampayuttā abhijjhādayo vuttā.
rows.english: But with the words also all kamma that leads to becoming the covetousness, etc., associated with volition are expressed.

rows.pali: Upapattibhavo pana saṅkhepato kammābhinibbattā khandhā, pabhedato navavidho hoti.
rows.english: 253. Rebirth-process becoming briefly is aggregates generated by kamma. It is of nine kinds,

rows.pali: Yathāha – "tattha katamo upapattibhavo?
rows.english: according as it is said: “Herein, what is rebirth-process becoming?

rows.pali: Kāmabhavo rūpabhavo arūpabhavo saññābhavo asaññābhavo nevasaññānāsaññābhavo, ekavokārabhavo catuvokārabhavo pañcavokārabhavo, ayaṃ vuccati upapattibhavo"ti (vibha. 234).
rows.english: Sense-desire becoming, fine-material becoming, immaterial becoming, percipient becoming, non-percipient becoming, neither-percipient-nor-non-percipient becoming, one-constituent becoming, [572] four-constituent becoming, five- constituent becoming: this is called rebirth-process becoming” (Vibh 17).

rows.pali: Tattha kāmasaṅkhāto bhavo kāmabhavo.
rows.english: 254. Herein, the kind of becoming called “having sense desires” is sense-desire becoming.

rows.pali: Esa nayo rūpārūpabhavesu.
rows.english: Similarly with the fine-material and immaterial kinds of becoming.

rows.pali: Saññāvataṃ bhavo, saññā vā ettha bhave atthīti saññābhavo.
rows.english: It is the becoming of those possessed of perception, or there is perception here in becoming, thus it is percipient becoming.

rows.pali: Vipariyāyena asaññābhavo.
rows.english: The opposite kind is non-percipient becoming.

rows.pali: Oḷārikāya saññāya abhāvā sukhumāya ca bhāvā nevasaññā, nāsaññā asmiṃ bhaveti nevasaññānāsaññābhavo.
rows.english: Owing to the absence of gross perception and to the presence of subtle perception there is neither perception nor non-perception in that kind of becoming, thus it is neither-percipient-nor-non-percipient becoming.

rows.pali: Ekena rūpakkhandhena vokiṇṇo bhavo ekavokārabhavo.
rows.english: It is becoming constituted out of the materiality aggregate only, thus it is one-constituent becoming,

rows.pali: Eko vā vokāro assa bhavassāti ekavokārabhavo.
rows.english: or that kind of becoming has only one constituent, [the materiality aggregate, or dimension,] thus it is one-constituent becoming.

rows.pali: Esa nayo catuvokārapañcavokārabhavesu.
rows.english: And similarly the four-constituent [has the four mental aggregates, or dimensions,] and the five-constituent [has the material and the four mental aggregates, or dimensions].

rows.pali: Tattha kāmabhavo pañca upādiṇṇakkhandhā.
rows.english: 255. Herein, sense-desire becoming is five aggregates acquired through kamma (clung to).

rows.pali: Tathā rūpabhavo.
rows.english: Likewise the fine-material becoming.

rows.pali: Arūpabhavo cattāro, saññābhavo pañca.
rows.english: Immaterial becoming is four. Percipient becoming is four and five.

rows.pali: Asaññābhavo eko upādiṇṇakkhandho.
rows.english: Non-percipient becoming is one aggregate that is acquired through kamma (clung to).

rows.pali: Nevasaññānāsaññābhavo cattāro.
rows.english: Neither-percipient-nor-non-percipient becoming is four.

rows.pali: Ekavokārabhavādayo ekacatupañcakkhandhā upādiṇṇakkhandhehīti evamettha dhammatopi viññātabbo vinicchayo.
rows.english: One-constituent becoming, etc., are respectively one, four, and five aggregates as aggregates that are acquired through kamma (clung to).

rows.pali: 648.Sātthatoti yathā ca bhavaniddese, tatheva kāmaṃ saṅkhāraniddesepi puññābhisaṅkhārādayova vuttā, evaṃ santepi purime atītakammavasena idha paṭisandhiyā paccayattā, ime paccuppannakammavasena āyatiṃ paṭisandhiyā paccayattāti punavacanaṃ sātthakameva, pubbe vā "tattha katamo puññābhisaṅkhāro?
rows.english: This is how the exposition should be known here “as to state. ” 256. 3. As to purpose: although formations of merit, etc., are of course dealt with in the same way in the description of becoming and in the description of formations (see Vibh 135, 137), nevertheless the repetition has a purpose. For in the former case it was because it was a condition, as past kamma, for rebirth- linking here [in this becoming], while in the latter case it is because it is a condition, as present kamma, for rebirth-linking in the future [becoming]. Or alternatively, in the former instance, in the passage beginning, “Herein, what is the formation of merit?

rows.pali: Kusalā cetanā kāmāvacarā"ti (vibha. 226) evamādinā nayena cetanāva saṅkhārāti vuttā.
rows.english: It is profitable volition of the sense sphere” (Vibh 135), it was only volitions that were called “formations”;

rows.pali: Idha pana "sabbampi bhavagāmikamma"nti (vibha. 234) vacanato cetanāsampayuttāpi.
rows.english: but here, with the words “All kamma that leads to becoming” (Vibh 137), the states associated with the volition are also included.

rows.pali: Pubbe ca viññāṇapaccayameva kammaṃ "saṅkhārā"ti vuttaṃ.
rows.english: And in the former instance it was only such kamma as is a condition for consciousness that was called ‘formations’;

rows.pali: Idāni asaññābhavanibbattakampi.
rows.english: but now also that which generates non-percipient becoming is included.

rows.pali: Kiṃ vā bahunā, "avijjāpaccayā saṅkhārā"ti ettha puññābhisaṅkhārādayova kusalākusalā dhammā vuttā.
rows.english: 257. But why so many words? In the clause “With ignorance as condition there are formations,” only profitable and unprofitable states are expressed as the formation of merit, etc.;

rows.pali: "Upādānapaccayā bhavo"ti idha pana upapattibhavassāpi saṅgahitattā kusalākusalābyākatā dhammā vuttā.
rows.english: but in the clause “With clinging as condition, becoming,” profitable and unprofitable and also functional states are expressed because of the inclusion of rebirth-process becoming.

rows.pali: Tasmā sabbathāpi sātthakamevidaṃ punavacananti evamettha sātthatopi viññātabbo vinicchayo.
rows.english: So this repetition has a purpose in each case. This is how the exposition should be known “as to purpose here. ”

rows.pali: 649.Bhedasaṅgahāti upādānapaccayā bhavassa bhedato ceva saṅgahato ca.
rows.english: 258. 4. As to analysis, synthesis means as to both the analysis and the synthesis of becoming that has clinging as its condition.

rows.pali: Yañhi kāmupādānapaccayā kāmabhavanibbattakaṃ kammaṃ karīyati, so kammabhavo.
rows.english: The kamma with sense-desire clinging as its condition that is performed and generates sense-desire becoming is “kamma-process becoming.”

rows.pali: Tadabhinibbattā khandhā upapattibhavo.
rows.english: The aggregates generated by that are “rebirth- process becoming”;

rows.pali: Esa nayo rūpārūpabhavesu.
rows.english: similarly in the case of fine-material and immaterial becoming.

rows.pali: Evaṃ kāmupādānapaccayā dve kāmabhavā, tadantogadhā ca saññābhavapañcavokārabhavā, dve rūpabhavā, tadantogadhā ca saññābhavaasaññābhavaekavokārabhavapañcavokārabhavā, dve arūpabhavā, tadantogadhā ca saññābhavanevasaññānāsaññābhavacatuvokārabhavāti saddhiṃ antogadhehi cha bhavā.
rows.english: So [573] there are two kinds of sense-desire becoming with sense- desire clinging as condition, included in which are percipient becoming and five-constituent becoming. And there are two kinds of fine-material becoming, included in which are percipient, non-percipient, one-constituent, and five- constituent becoming. And there are two kinds of immaterial becoming, included in which are percipient becoming, neither-percipient-nor-non-percipient becoming, and four-constituent becoming.

rows.pali: Yathā ca kāmupādānapaccayā saddhiṃ antogadhehi cha bhavā.
rows.english: So, together with what is included by them, there are six kinds of becoming with sense-desire clinging as condition.

rows.pali: Tathā sesupādānapaccayāpīti evaṃ upādānapaccayā bhedato saddhiṃ antogadhehi catuvīsati bhavā.
rows.english: Similarly too with the [three] remaining kinds of clinging as condition. So, as to analysis, there are, together with what is included by them, twenty-four kinds of becoming with clinging as condition.

rows.pali: Saṅgahato pana kammabhavaṃ upapattibhavañca ekato katvā kāmupādānapaccayā saddhiṃ antogadhehi eko kāmabhavo.
rows.english: 259.5. As to synthesis, however, by uniting kamma-process becoming and rebirth- process becoming there is, together with what is included by it, one kind of sense-desire becoming with sense-desire clinging as its condition.

rows.pali: Tathā rūpārūpabhavāti tayo bhavā.
rows.english: Similarly with fine-material and immaterial becoming. So there are three kinds of becoming.

rows.pali: Tathā sesupādānapaccayā pīti.
rows.english: And similarly with the remaining [three] kinds of clinging as condition.

rows.pali: Evaṃ upādānapaccayā saṅgahato saddhiṃ antogadhehi dvādasa bhavā.
rows.english: So by synthesis, there are, together with what is included by them, twelve kinds of becoming with clinging as condition.

rows.pali: Apica avisesena upādānapaccayā kāmabhavūpagaṃ kammaṃ kammabhavo.
rows.english: 260. Furthermore, without distinction the kamma with clinging as its condition that attains sense-desire becoming is kamma-process becoming.

rows.pali: Tadabhinibbattā khandhā upapattibhavo.
rows.english: The aggregates generated by that are rebirth-process becoming.

rows.pali: Esa nayo rūpārūpabhavesu.
rows.english: Similarly in the fine-material and immaterial becoming.

rows.pali: Evaṃ upādānapaccayā saddhiṃ antogadhehi dve kāmabhavā, dve rūpabhavā, dve arūpabhavāti aparena pariyāyena saṅgahato cha bhavā.
rows.english: So, together with what is included by them, there are two kinds of sense-desire becoming, two kinds of fine-material becoming, and two kinds of immaterial becoming. So, by synthesis, there are six kinds of becoming by this other method.

rows.pali: Kammabhavaupapattibhavabhedaṃ vā anupagamma saddhiṃ antogadhehi kāmabhavādivasena tayo bhavā honti.
rows.english: Or again, without making the division into kamma-process becoming and rebirth-process becoming, there are, together with what is included by them, three kinds of becoming as sense-desire becoming, and so on.

rows.pali: Kāmabhavādibhedampi anupagamma kammabhavaupapattibhavavasena dve bhavā honti.
rows.english: Or again, without making the division into sense-desire becoming, etc., there are, together with what is included by them, two kinds of becoming, as kamma-process becoming and rebirth-process becoming.

rows.pali: Kammupapattibhedañcāpi anupagamma upādānapaccayā bhavoti bhavavasena ekova bhavo hotīti evamettha upādānapaccayassa bhavassa bhedasaṅgahāpi viññātabbo vinicchayo.
rows.english: And also without making the division into kamma process and rebirth process there is, according to the words “With clinging as condition, becoming,” only one kind of becoming. This is how the exposition of becoming with clinging as condition should be known here “as to analysis and synthesis. ”

rows.pali: 650.Yaṃ yassa paccayo cevāti yañcettha upādānaṃ yassa paccayo hoti, tatopi viññātabbo vinicchayoti attho.
rows.english: 261. 6. Which for which becomes condition means that here the exposition should be known according to what kind of clinging is a condition for what [kind of becoming].

rows.pali: Kiṃ panettha kassa paccayo hoti?
rows.english: But what is condition for what here?

rows.pali: Yaṃkiñci yassa kassaci paccayo hotiyeva.
rows.english: Any kind is a condition for any kind.

rows.pali: Ummattako viya hi puthujjano.
rows.english: For the ordinary man is like a madman,

rows.pali: So idaṃ yuttaṃ idaṃ ayuttanti avicāretvā yassa kassaci upādānassa vasena yaṃkiñci bhavaṃ patthetvā yaṃkiñci kammaṃ karotiyeva.
rows.english: and without considering “Is this right or not?” and aspiring by means of any of the kinds of clinging to any of the kinds of becoming, he performs any of the kinds of kamma.

rows.pali: Tasmā yadekacce sīlabbatupādānena rūpārūpabhavā na hontīti vadanti, taṃ na gahetabbaṃ.
rows.english: Therefore when some say that the fine-material and immaterial kinds of becoming do not come about through rules-and-vows clinging, that should not be accepted:

rows.pali: Sabbena pana sabbo hotīti gahetabbaṃ.
rows.english: what should be accepted is that all kinds come about through all kinds.

rows.pali: Seyyathidaṃ – idhekacco anussavavasena vā diṭṭhānusārena vā "kāmā nāmete manussaloke ceva khattiyamahāsālakulādīsu, cha kāmāvacaradevaloke ca samiddhā"ti cintetvā tesaṃ adhigamatthaṃ asaddhammassavanādīhi vañcito "iminā kammena kāmā sampajjantī"ti maññamāno kāmupādānavasena kāyaduccaritādīnipi karoti, so duccaritapāripūriyā apāye upapajjati.
rows.english: 262. For example, someone thinks in accordance with hearsay or [false] view that sense desires come to be fulfilled in the human world among the great warrior (khattiya) families, etc., and in the six divine worlds of the sense sphere. [574] Misled by listening to wrong doctrine, etc., and imagining that “by this kamma sense desires will come to be fulfilled,” he performs for the purpose of attaining them acts of bodily misconduct, etc., through sense-desire clinging. By fulfilling such misconduct he is reborn in the states of loss.

rows.pali: Sandiṭṭhike vā pana kāme patthayamāno paṭiladdhe ca gopayamāno kāmupādānavasena kāyaduccaritādīni karoti, so duccaritapāripūriyā apāye upapajjati.
rows.english: Or he performs acts of bodily misconduct, etc., aspiring to sense desires visible here and now and protecting those he has already acquired. By fulfilling such misconduct he is reborn in the states of loss.

rows.pali: Tatrāssa upapattihetubhūtaṃ kammaṃ kammabhavo.
rows.english: The kamma that is the cause of rebirth there is kamma-process becoming.

rows.pali: Kammābhinibbattā khandhā upapattibhavo.
rows.english: The aggregates generated by the kamma are rebirth- process becoming.

rows.pali: Saññābhavapañcavokārabhavā pana tadantogadhā eva.
rows.english: But percipient becoming and five-constituent becoming are included in that, too.

rows.pali: Aparo pana saddhammassavanādīhi upabrūhitañāṇo "iminā kammena kāmā sampajjantī"ti maññamāno kāmupādānavasena kāyasucaritādīni karoti.
rows.english: 263. Another, however, whose knowledge has been intensified by listening to good Dhamma and so on, imagines that “by this kind of kamma sense desires will come to be fulfilled. ” He performs acts of bodily good conduct, etc., through sense-desire clinging.

rows.pali: So sucaritapāripūriyā devesu vā manussesu vā upapajjati.
rows.english: By fulfilling such bodily good conduct he is reborn among deities or human beings.

rows.pali: Tatrāssa upapattihetubhūtaṃ kammaṃ kammabhavo.
rows.english: The kamma that is the cause of his rebirth there is kamma-process becoming.

rows.pali: Kammābhinibbattā khandhā upapattibhavo.
rows.english: The aggregates generated by the kamma are rebirth- process becoming.

rows.pali: Saññābhavapañcavokārabhavā pana tadantogadhā eva.
rows.english: But percipient becoming and five-constituent becoming are included in that, too.

rows.pali: Iti kāmupādānaṃ sappabhedassa sāntogadhassa kāmabhavassa paccayo hoti.
rows.english: So sense-desire clinging is a condition for sense-desire becoming with its analysis and its synthesis.

rows.pali: Aparo "rūpārūpabhavesu tato samiddhatarā kāmā"ti sutvā parikappetvā vā kāmupādānavaseneva rūpārūpasamāpattiyo nibbattetvā samāpattibalena rūpārūpabrahmaloke upapajjati.
rows.english: 264. Another hears or conjectures that sense desires come to still greater perfection in the fine-material and immaterial kinds of becoming, and through sense-desire clinging he produces the fine-material and immaterial attainments, and in virtue of his attainments he is reborn in the fine-material or immaterial Brahmā-world.

rows.pali: Tatrāssa upapattihetubhūtaṃ kammaṃ kammabhavo.
rows.english: The kamma that is the cause of his rebirth there is kamma-process becoming.

rows.pali: Kammābhinibbattā khandhā upapattibhavo.
rows.english: The aggregates generated by the kamma are rebirth-process becoming.

rows.pali: Saññā-asaññā-nevasaññānāsaññā-eka-catu-pañcavokārabhavā pana tadantogadhā eva.
rows.english: But percipient, non-percipient, neither-percipient-nor-non-percipient, one- constituent, four-constituent, and five-constituent kinds of becoming are included in that, too.

rows.pali: Iti kāmupādānaṃ sappabhedānaṃ sāntogadhānaṃ rūpārūpabhavānampi paccayo hoti.
rows.english: Thus sense-desire clinging is a condition for fine-material and immaterial becoming with its analysis and its synthesis.

rows.pali: Aparo "ayaṃ attā nāma kāmāvacarasampattibhave vā rūpārūpabhavānaṃ vā aññatarasmiṃ ucchinne suucchinno hotī"ti ucchedadiṭṭhiṃ upādāya tadupagaṃ kammaṃ karoti, tassa taṃ kammaṃ kammabhavo.
rows.english: 265. Another clings to the annihilation view thus: “This self comes to be entirely cut off when it is cut off in the fortunate states of the sense sphere, or in the fine- material or immaterial kinds of becoming,” and he performs kamma to achieve that. His kamma is kamma-process becoming.

rows.pali: Kammābhinibbattā khandhā upapattibhavo.
rows.english: The aggregates generated by the kamma are rebirth-process becoming.

rows.pali: Saññābhavādayo pana tadantogadhā eva.
rows.english: But the percipient, etc., kinds of becoming are included in that too.

rows.pali: Iti diṭṭhupādānaṃ sappabhedānaṃ sāntogadhānaṃ tiṇṇampi kāmarūpārūpabhavānaṃ paccayo hoti.
rows.english: So [false-]view clinging is a condition for all three, namely, for the sense-desire, fine-material, and immaterial kinds of becoming with their analysis and their synthesis.

rows.pali: Aparo "ayaṃ attā nāma kāmāvacarasampattibhave vā rūpārūpabhavānaṃ vā aññatarasmiṃ sukhī hoti vigatapariḷāho"ti attavādupādānena tadupagaṃ kammaṃ karoti, tassa taṃ kammaṃ kammabhavo.
rows.english: 266. Another through self-theory clinging thinks, “This self comes to be blissful, or comes to be free from fever, in the becoming in the fortunate states in the sense sphere or in one or other of the fine-material and immaterial kinds of becoming,” and he performs kamma to achieve that. That kamma of his is kamma- process becoming.

rows.pali: Tadabhinibbattā khandhā upapattibhavo.
rows.english: The aggregates generated by the kamma are rebirth- process becoming.

rows.pali: Saññābhavādayo pana tadantogadhā eva.
rows.english: But the percipient, etc., kinds of becoming are included in that, too.

rows.pali: Iti attavādupādānaṃ sappabhedānaṃ sāntogadhānaṃ tiṇṇaṃ bhavānaṃ paccayo hoti.
rows.english: Thus this self-theory clinging is a condition for all the three, namely, becoming with their analysis and their synthesis.

rows.pali: Aparo "idaṃ sīlabbataṃ nāma kāmāvacarasampattibhave vā rūpārūpabhavānaṃ vā aññatarasmiṃ paripūrentassa sukhaṃ pāripūriṃ gacchatī"ti sīlabbatupādānavasena tadupagaṃ kammaṃ karoti, tassa taṃ kammaṃ kammabhavo.
rows.english: 267. Another [thinks] through rules-and-vows clinging, “This rite and ritual leads him who perfects it to perfect bliss in becoming in the fortunate states of the sense sphere or in the fine-material or immaterial kinds of becoming,” and he performs kamma to achieve that. That kamma of his is kamma-process becoming.

rows.pali: Tadabhinibbattā khandhā upapattibhavo.
rows.english: The aggregates generated by the kamma are rebirth-process becoming.

rows.pali: Saññābhavādayo pana tadantogadhā eva.
rows.english: But the percipient, etc., kinds of becoming are included in that, too.

rows.pali: Iti sīlabbatupādānaṃ sappabhedānaṃ sāntogadhānaṃ tiṇṇaṃ bhavānaṃ paccayo hoti.
rows.english: So rules-and- vows clinging is a condition for all three, namely, the sense-desire, fine-material and immaterial kinds of becoming with their analysis and their synthesis.

rows.pali: Evamettha yaṃ yassa paccayo hoti, tatopi viññātabbo vinicchayo.
rows.english: This is how the exposition should be known here according to “which is condition for which. ”

rows.pali: Kiṃ panettha kassa bhavassa kathaṃ paccayo hotīti ce?
rows.english: 268. But which is condition for which kind of becoming in what way here?

rows.pali: Rūpārūpabhavānaṃ hi, kāmabhavapariyāpannassa ca kammabhave kusalakammasseva, upapattibhavassa cetaṃ catubbidhampi upādānaṃ upanissayapaccayavasena ekadhāva paccayo hoti.
rows.english: 269.This clinging, though fourfold, is a condition in only one way as decisive- support condition for becoming both fine-material and immaterial, [that is,] for the profitable kamma in the kamma-process becoming that takes place in sense-desire becoming and for the rebirth-process becoming.

rows.pali: Kāmabhave attanā sampayuttākusalakammabhavassa sahajātaaññamaññanissayasampayuttaatthiavigatahetupaccayappabhedehi sahajātādīhi paccayo hoti.
rows.english: It is a condition, as conascence and so on, that is, as conascence, mutuality, support, association, presence, non- disappearance, and root-cause conditions, for the unprofitable kamma-process becoming associated with [the fourfold clinging] itself in the sense-desire becoming.

rows.pali: Vippayuttassa pana upanissayapaccayenevāti.
rows.english: But it is a condition, as decisive-support only, for that which is dissociated.

rows.pali: 651.Bhavapaccayā jātītiādīsu jātiādīnaṃ vinicchayo saccaniddese vuttanayeneva veditabbo.
rows.english: 270. As regards the clause “With becoming as condition, birth,” etc., the definition of birth should be understood in the way given in the Description of the Truths (XIV.31ff.)

rows.pali: Bhavoti panettha kammabhavova adhippeto.
rows.english: Only kamma-process becoming is intended here as “becoming”;

rows.pali: So hi jātiyā paccayo, na upapattibhavo.
rows.english: for it is that, not rebirth-process becoming, which is a condition for birth.

rows.pali: So ca pana kammapaccayaupanissayapaccayavasena dvedhā paccayo hotīti.
rows.english: But it is a condition in two ways, as kamma condition and as decisive-support condition.

rows.pali: Tattha siyā – kathaṃ panetaṃ jānitabbaṃ bhavo jātiyā paccayoti ce?
rows.english: 271. Here it may be asked: “But how is it to be known that becoming is a condition for birth?”

rows.pali: Bāhirapaccayasamattepi hīnapaṇītatādivisesadassanato.
rows.english: Because of the observable difference of inferiority and superiority.

rows.pali: Bāhirānaṃ hi janakajananīsukkasoṇitāhārādīnaṃ paccayānaṃ samattepi sattānaṃ yamakānampi sataṃ hīnapaṇītatādiviseso dissati.
rows.english: For in spite of equality of external circumstances, such as father, mother, seed, blood, nutriment, etc., the difference of inferiority and superiority of beings is observable even in the case of twins.

rows.pali: So ca na ahetuko sabbadā ca sabbesañca abhāvato, na kammabhavato aññahetuko tadabhinibbattakasattānaṃ ajjhattasantāne aññassa kāraṇassa abhāvatoti kammabhavahetukova.
rows.english: And that fact is not causeless, since it is not present always and in all; nor has it any cause other than kamma-process becoming since there is no other reason in the internal continuity of beings generated by it. Consequently, it has only kamma-process becoming for its cause.

rows.pali: Kammaṃ hi sattānaṃ hīnapaṇītatādivisesassa hetu.
rows.english: And because kamma is the cause of the difference of inferiority and superiority among beings

rows.pali: Tenāha bhagavā "kammaṃ satte vibhajati yadidaṃ hīnappaṇītatāyā"ti (ma. ni. 3.289).
rows.english: the Blessed One said, “It is kamma that separates beings according to inferiority and superiority” (M III 203).

rows.pali: Tasmā jānitabbametaṃ "bhavo jātiyā paccayo"ti.
rows.english: From that it can be known that becoming is a condition for birth.

rows.pali: Yasmā ca asati jātiyā jarāmaraṇaṃ nāma, sokādayo vā dhammā na honti.
rows.english: 272.And when there is no birth, neither ageing and death nor the states beginning with sorrow come about;

rows.pali: Jātiyā pana sati jarāmaraṇañceva, jarāmaraṇasaṅkhātadukkhadhammaphuṭṭhassa ca bālajanassa jarāmaraṇābhisambandhā vā tena tena dukkhadhammena phuṭṭhassa anabhisambandhā vā sokādayo ca dhammā honti.
rows.english: but when there is birth, then ageing and death come about, and also the states beginning with sorrow, which are either bound up with ageing and death in a fool who is affected by the painful states called ageing and death, or which are not so bound up in one who is affected by some painful state or other;

rows.pali: Tasmā ayampi jāti jarāmaraṇassa ceva sokādīnañca paccayo hotīti veditabbā.
rows.english: therefore this birth is a condition for ageing and death and also for sorrow and so on.

rows.pali: Sā pana upanissayakoṭiyā ekadhāva paccayo hotīti.
rows.english: But it is a condition in one way, as decisive-support type.

rows.pali: 652.Yasmā panettha sokādayo avasāne vuttā, tasmā yā sā avijjāpaccayā saṅkhārāti evametassa bhavacakkassa ādimhi vuttā, sā,
rows.english: 273. Now, here at the end sorrow, etc., are stated. Consequently, the ignorance stated at the beginning of the Wheel of Becoming thus, “With ignorance as condition there are formations,”

rows.pali: Satataṃ samitaṃ pavattatīti veditabbaṃ.
rows.english: and nowhere It ever halts; forever it is spinning: so it should accordingly be understood.

rows.pali: Kathaṃ panettha sokādīhi avijjā siddhā, kathamidaṃ bhavacakkaṃ aviditādi, kathaṃ kārakavedakarahitaṃ, kathaṃ dvādasavidhasuññatāsuññanti ce?
rows.english: 274. But (1) how is ignorance established by sorrow, etc.? (2) How has this Wheel of Becoming no known beginning? (3) How is there no maker or experiencer there? (4) How is it void with twelvefold voidness?

rows.pali: Ettha hi sokadomanassupāyāsā avijjāya aviyogino, paridevo ca nāma mūḷhassāti tesu tāva siddhesu siddhā hoti avijjā.
rows.english: 275. 1. Sorrow, grief and despair are inseparable from ignorance; and lamentation is found in one who is deluded. So, firstly, when these are established, ignorance is established.

rows.pali: Apica "āsavasamudayā avijjāsamudayo"ti (ma. ni. 1.103) vuttaṃ.
rows.english: Furthermore, “With the arising of cankers there is the arising of ignorance” (M I 54) is said,

rows.pali: Āsavasamudayā cete sokādayo honti.
rows.english: and with the arising of cankers these things beginning with sorrow come into being.

rows.pali: Kathaṃ?
rows.english: How?

rows.pali: Vatthukāmaviyoge tāva soko kāmāsavasamudayā hoti.
rows.english: 276. Firstly, sorrow about separation from sense desires as object has its arising in the canker of sense desire,

rows.pali: Yathāha –
rows.english: according as it is said:

rows.pali: Yathā cāha – "kāmato jāyati soko"ti. (Dha. pa. 215).
rows.english: and according as it is said: “Sorrow springs from sense desires” (Dhp 215).

rows.pali: Sabbepi cete diṭṭhāsavasamudayā honti.
rows.english: 277. And all these come about with the arising of the canker of views,

rows.pali: Yathāha –
rows.english: according as it is said:

rows.pali: "Tassa 'ahaṃ rūpaṃ mama rūpa'nti pariyuṭṭhaṭṭhāyino rūpavipariṇāmaññathābhāvā uppajjanti sokaparidevadukkhadomanassupāyāsā"ti (saṃ. ni. 3.1).
rows.english: “In one who [577] possesses [the view] ‘I am materiality,’ ‘my materiality,’ with the change and transformation of materiality there arise sorrow and lamentation, pain, grief and despair” (S III 3).

rows.pali: Yathā ca diṭṭhāsavasamudayā, evaṃ bhavāsavasamudayāpi.
rows.english: 278. And as with the arising of the canker of views, so also with the arising of the canker of becoming,

rows.pali: Yathāha –
rows.english: according as it is said:

rows.pali: "Yepi te devā dīghāyukā vaṇṇavanto sukhabahulā uccesu vimānesu ciraṭṭhitikā, tepi tathāgatassa dhammadesanaṃ sutvā bhayaṃ santāsaṃ saṃvegamāpajjantī"ti (saṃ. ni. 3.78).
rows.english: “Then whatever deities there are, long-lived, beautiful, blissful, long-resident in grand palaces, when they hear the Perfect One’s teaching of the Dhamma, they feel fear, anxiety and a sense of urgency” (S III 85),

rows.pali: Pañca pubbanimittāni disvā maraṇabhayena santajjitānaṃ devānaṃ viya.
rows.english: as in the case of deities harassed by the fear of death on seeing the five signs. 43

rows.pali: Yathā ca bhavāsavasamudayā, evaṃ avijjāsavasamudayāpi.
rows.english: 279. And as with the arising of the canker of becoming, so also with the canker of ignorance,

rows.pali: Yathāha –
rows.english: according as it is said:

rows.pali: "Sa kho so, bhikkhave, bālo tividhaṃ diṭṭheva dhamme dukkhaṃ domanassaṃ paṭisaṃvedetī"ti (ma. ni. 3.246).
rows.english: “The fool, bhikkhus, experiences pain and grief here and now in three ways” (M III 163).

rows.pali: Iti yasmā āsavasamudayā ete dhammā honti, tasmā ete sijjhamānā avijjāya hetubhūte āsave sādhenti.
rows.english: Now, these states come about with the arising of cankers, and so when they are established, they establish the cankers which are the cause of ignorance.

rows.pali: Āsavesu ca siddhesu paccayabhāve bhāvato avijjāpi siddhāva hotīti.
rows.english: And when the cankers are established, ignorance is also established because it is present when its condition is present.

rows.pali: Evaṃ tāvettha sokādīhi avijjā siddhā hotīti veditabbā.
rows.english: This, in the first place, is how ignorance, etc., should be understood to be established by sorrow and so on.

rows.pali: Yasmā pana evaṃ paccayabhāve bhāvato avijjāya siddhāya puna avijjāpaccayā saṅkhārā, saṅkhārapaccayā viññāṇanti evaṃ hetuphalaparamparāya pariyosānaṃ natthi.
rows.english: 280. 2. But when ignorance is established since it is present when its condition is present, and when “with ignorance as condition there are formations; with formations as condition, consciousness,” there is no end to the succession of cause with fruit in this way.

rows.pali: Tasmā taṃ hetuphalasambandhavasena pavattaṃ dvādasaṅgaṃ bhavacakkaṃ aviditādīti siddhaṃ hoti.
rows.english: Consequently, the Wheel of Becoming with its twelve factors, revolving with the linking of cause and effect, is established as having “no known beginning.”

rows.pali: Evaṃ sati avijjāpaccayā saṅkhārāti idaṃ ādimattakathanaṃ virujjhatīti ce.
rows.english: 281. This being so, are not the words “With ignorance as condition there are formations,” as an exposition of a simple beginning, contradicted?

rows.pali: Nayidaṃ ādimattakathanaṃ.
rows.english: —This is not an exposition of a simple beginning.

rows.pali: Padhānadhammakathanaṃ panetaṃ.
rows.english: It is an exposition of a basic state (see §107).

rows.pali: Tiṇṇannaṃ hi vaṭṭānaṃ avijjā padhānā.
rows.english: For ignorance is the basic state for the three rounds (see §298).

rows.pali: Avijjāggahaṇena hi avasesakilesavaṭṭañca kammādīni ca bālaṃ palibodhenti.
rows.english: It is owing to his seizing ignorance that the fool gets caught in the round of the remaining defilements, in the rounds of kamma, etc.,

rows.pali: Sappasiraggahaṇena sesasappasarīraṃ viya bāhaṃ.
rows.english: just as it is owing to seizing a snake’s head that the arm gets caught in [the coils of] the rest of the snake’s body.

rows.pali: Avijjāsamucchede pana kate tehi vimokkho hoti.
rows.english: But when the cutting off of ignorance is effected, he is liberated from them

rows.pali: Sappasiracchede kate palibodhitabāhāvimokkho viya.
rows.english: just as the arm caught [in the coils] is liberated when the snake’s head is cut off,

rows.pali: Yathāha – "avijjāyatveva asesavirāganirodhā saṅkhāranirodho"tiādi (saṃ. ni. 2.1; mahāva. 1).
rows.english: according as it is said, “With the remainderless fading away and cessation of ignorance” (S II 1), and so on.

rows.pali: Iti yaṃ gaṇhato bandho, muccato ca mokkho hoti, tassa padhānadhammassa kathanamidaṃ, na ādimattakathananti.
rows.english: So this is an exposition of the basic state whereby there is bondage for him who grasps it, and liberation for him who lets it go: it is not an exposition of a simple beginning.

rows.pali: Evamidaṃ bhavacakkaṃ aviditādīti veditabbaṃ.
rows.english: This is how the Wheel of Becoming should be understood to have no known beginning.

rows.pali: Tayidaṃ yasmā avijjādīhi kāraṇehi saṅkhārādīnaṃ pavatti, tasmā tato aññena "brahmā mahābrahmā seṭṭho sajitā"ti (dī. ni. 1.42) evaṃ parikappitena brahmādinā vā saṃsārassa kārakena, "so kho pana me ayaṃ attā vado vedeyyo"ti evaṃ parikappitena attanā vā sukhadukkhānaṃ vedakena rahitaṃ.
rows.english: 282. 3. This Wheel of Becoming consists in the occurrence of formations, etc., with ignorance, etc., as the respective reasons. Therefore it is devoid of a maker supplementary to that, such as a Brahmā conjectured thus, “Brahmā the Great, the Highest, the Creator” (D I 18), to perform the function of maker of the round of rebirths; and it is devoid of any self as an experiencer of pleasure and pain conceived thus, “This self of mine that speaks and feels” (cf. M I 8).

rows.pali: Iti kārakavedakarahitanti veditabbaṃ.
rows.english: This is how it should be understood to be without any maker or experiencer.

rows.pali: Yasmā panettha avijjā udayabbayadhammakattā dhuvabhāvena, saṃkiliṭṭhattā saṃkilesikattā ca subhabhāvena, udayabbayapaṭipīḷitattā sukhabhāvena, paccayāyattavuttittā vasavattanabhūtena attabhāvena ca suññā.
rows.english: 283. 4. However, ignorance is void of lastingness since its nature is to rise and fall, and it is void of beauty since it is defiled and causes defilement, and it is void of pleasure since it is oppressed by rise and fall, and it is void of any selfhood susceptible to the wielding of power since it exists in dependence on conditions.

rows.pali: Tathā saṅkhārādīnipi aṅgāni.
rows.english: and likewise the factors consisting of formations, etc.

rows.pali: Yasmā vā avijjā na attā, na attano, na attani, na attavatī.
rows.english: Or ignorance is neither self nor self’s nor in self nor possessed of self.

rows.pali: Tathā saṅkhārādīnipi aṅgāni.
rows.english: And likewise the factors consisting of formations, etc.

rows.pali: Tasmā dvādasavidhasuññatāsuññametaṃ bhavacakkanti veditabbaṃ.
rows.english: That is why this Wheel of Becoming should be understood thus, “Void with a twelvefold voidness.”

rows.pali: 653.Evañca viditvā puna,
rows.english: 284. After knowing this, again:

rows.pali: Tassa kho panetassa bhavacakkassa avijjā taṇhā cāti dve dhammā mūlanti veditabbā.
rows.english: 285. The two things, ignorance and craving, should be understood as the root of this Wheel of Becoming.

rows.pali: Tadetaṃ pubbantāharaṇato avijjāmūlaṃ vedanāvasānaṃ, aparantasantānato taṇhāmūlaṃ jarāmaraṇāvasānanti duvidhaṃ hoti.
rows.english: Of the derivation from the past, ignorance is the root and feeling the end. And of the continuation into the future, craving is the root and ageing-and-death the end. It is twofold in this way.

rows.pali: Tattha purimaṃ diṭṭhicaritavasena vuttaṃ, pacchimaṃ taṇhācaritavasena.
rows.english: 286. Herein, the first applies to one whose temperament is [false] view, and the second to one whose temperament is craving.

rows.pali: Diṭṭhicaritānaṃ hi avijjā, taṇhācaritānañca taṇhā saṃsāranāyikā.
rows.english: For in the round of rebirths ignorance leads those whose temperament favours [false] view, and craving those whose temperament favours craving.

rows.pali: Ucchedadiṭṭhisamugghātāya vā paṭhamaṃ, phaluppattiyā hetūnaṃ anupacchedappakāsanato, sassatadiṭṭhisamugghātāya dutiyaṃ, uppannānaṃ jarāmaraṇappakāsanato.
rows.english: Or the first has the purpose of eliminating the annihilation view because, by the evidence of the fruit, it proves that there is no annihilation of the causes; and the second has the purpose of eliminating the eternity view because it proves the ageing and death of whatever has arisen.

rows.pali: Gabbhaseyyakavasena vā purimaṃ, anupubbapavattidīpanato, opapātikavasena pacchimaṃ, sahuppattidīpanato.
rows.english: Or the first deals with the child in the womb because it illustrates successive occurrence [of the faculties], and the second deals with one apparitionally born because of [their] simultaneous appearance.

rows.pali: Atītapaccuppannānāgatā cassa tayo kālā.
rows.english: 287. The past, the present and the future are its three times.

rows.pali: Tesu pāḷiyaṃ sarūpato āgatavasena "avijjā, saṅkhārā cā"ti dve aṅgāni atītakālāni.
rows.english: Of these, it should be understood that, according to what is given as such in the texts, the two factors ignorance and formations belong to the past time,

rows.pali: Viññāṇādīni bhavāvasānāni aṭṭha paccuppannakālāni.
rows.english: the eight beginning with consciousness belong to the present time,

rows.pali: Jāti ceva jarāmaraṇañca dve anāgatakālānīti veditabbāni.
rows.english: and the two, birth and ageing- and-death, belong to the future time.

rows.pali: 654.Puna,
rows.english: 288.Again,

rows.pali: Itipi veditabbaṃ.
rows.english: - it should be understood thus.

rows.pali: Tattha saṅkhārānañca paṭisandhiviññāṇassa ca antarā eko hetuphalasandhi nāma.
rows.english: 289. 1. Herein, between formations and rebirth-linking consciousness there is one link consisting of cause-fruit.

rows.pali: Vedanāya ca taṇhāya ca antarā eko phalahetusandhi nāma.
rows.english: Between feeling and craving there is one link consisting of fruit-cause.

rows.pali: Bhavassa ca jātiyā ca antarā eko hetuphalasandhīti evamidaṃ hetuphalahetupubbakatisandhīti veditabbaṃ.
rows.english: And between becoming and birth there is one link consisting of cause-fruit. This is how it should be understood that it has three links with cause, fruit, cause, as first parts.

rows.pali: Sandhīnaṃ ādipariyosānavavatthitā panassa cattāro saṅgahā honti.
rows.english: 290. 2. But there are four sections, which are determined by the beginnings and ends of the links,

rows.pali: Seyyathidaṃ – avijjāsaṅkhārā eko saṅgaho.
rows.english: that is to say, ignorance/ formations is one section;

rows.pali: Viññāṇanāmarūpasaḷāyatanaphassavedanā dutiyo.
