rows.english: consciousness/mentality-materiality/ sixfold base/contact/feeling is the second;

rows.pali: Taṇhupādānabhavā tatiyo.
rows.english: craving/clinging/ becoming is the third;

rows.pali: Jātijarāmaraṇaṃ catutthoti.
rows.english: and birth/ageing-and-death is the fourth.

rows.pali: Evamidaṃ catubhedasaṅgahanti veditabbaṃ.
rows.english: This is how it should be understood to have four different sections.

rows.pali: Etehi pana vīsatiyā ākārasaṅkhātehi arehi vīsatiākārāranti veditabbaṃ.
rows.english: It is according to these twenty spokes called “qualities” that the words its spokes are twenty qualities should be understood.

rows.pali: Tattha atīte hetavo pañcāti avijjā saṅkhārā cāti ime tāva dve vuttā eva.
rows.english: 292.(a) Herein, [as regards the words] There were five causes in the past, firstly only these two, namely, ignorance and formations, are mentioned.

rows.pali: Yasmā pana avidvā paritassati, paritassito upādiyati, tassupādānapaccayā bhavo.
rows.english: But one who is ignorant hankers, and hankering, clings, and with his clinging as condition there is becoming;

rows.pali: Tasmā taṇhupādānabhavāpi gahitā honti.
rows.english: therefore craving, clinging and becoming are included as well.

rows.pali: Tenāha "purimakammabhavasmiṃ moho avijjā, āyūhanā saṅkhārā, nikanti taṇhā, upagamanaṃ upādānaṃ, cetanā bhavoti ime pañca dhammā purimakammabhavasmiṃ idha paṭisandhiyā paccayā"ti (paṭi. ma. 1.47).
rows.english: Hence it is said: “In the previous kamma-process becoming, there is delusion, which is ignorance; there is accumulation, which is formations; there is attachment, which is craving; there is embracing, which is clinging; there is volition, which is becoming; thus these five things in the previous kamma-process becoming are conditions for rebirth-linking here [in the present becoming]” (Paṭis I 52).

rows.pali: Tattha purimakammabhavasminti purime kammabhave, atītajātiyaṃ kammabhave kariyamāneti attho.
rows.english: 293. Herein, In the previous kamma-process becoming means in kamma-process becoming done in the previous birth.

rows.pali: Moho avijjāti yo tadā dukkhādīsu moho, yena mūḷho kammaṃ karoti, sā avijjā.
rows.english: There is delusion, which is ignorance means that the delusion that there then was about suffering, etc., deluded whereby the man did the kamma, was ignorance.

rows.pali: Āyūhanā saṅkhārāti taṃ kammaṃ karoto yā purimacetanāyo, yathā "dānaṃ dassāmī"ti cittaṃ uppādetvā māsampi saṃvaccharampi dānupakaraṇāni sajjentassa uppannā purimacetanāyo.
rows.english: There is accumulation, which is formations means the prior volitions arisen in one who prepares the things necessary for a gift during a month, perhaps, or a year after he has had the thought “I shall give a gift.”

rows.pali: Paṭiggāhakānaṃ pana hatthe dakkhiṇaṃ patiṭṭhāpayato cetanā bhavoti vuccati.
rows.english: But it is the volitions of one who is actually placing the offerings in the recipients’ hands that are called “becoming.”

rows.pali: Ekāvajjanesu vā chasu javanesu cetanā āyūhanā saṅkhārā nāma.
rows.english: Or alternatively, it is the volition that is accumulation in six of the impulsions of a single adverting that is called “formations,”

rows.pali: Sattame bhavo.
rows.english: and the seventh volition is called “becoming.”

rows.pali: Yā kāci vā pana cetanā bhavo.
rows.english: Or any kind of volition is called “becoming”

rows.pali: Sampayuttā āyūhanā saṅkhārā nāma.
rows.english: and the accumulations associated therewith are called “formations.”

rows.pali: Nikanti taṇhāti yā kammaṃ karontassa phale upapattibhave nikāmanā patthanā, sā taṇhā nāma.
rows.english: There is attachment, which is craving means that in one performing kamma, whatever attachment and aspiration there is for its fruit as rebirth-process-becoming is called craving.

rows.pali: Upagamanaṃ upādānanti yaṃ kammabhavassa paccayabhūtaṃ "idaṃ katvā asukasmiṃ nāma ṭhāne kāme sevissāmi ucchijjissāmī"tiādinā nayena pavattaṃ upagamanaṃ gahaṇaṃ parāmasanaṃ, idaṃ upādānaṃ nāma.
rows.english: There is embracing, which is clinging means that the embracing, the grasping, the adherence, which is a condition for kamma-process becoming and occurs thus, “By doing this I shall preserve, or I shall cut off, sense desire in such and such a place,” is called clinging.

rows.pali: Cetanā bhavoti āyūhanāvasāne vuttā cetanā bhavoti evamattho veditabbo.
rows.english: There is volition, which is becoming means the kind of volition stated already at the end of the [sentence dealing with] accumulation is becoming. This is how the meaning should be understood.

rows.pali: Idāni phalapañcakanti viññāṇādivedanāvasānaṃ pāḷiyaṃ āgatameva.
rows.english: 294.(b) And now there is a fivefold fruit (§291) means what is given in the text beginning with consciousness and ending with feeling,

rows.pali: Yathāha – "idha paṭisandhi viññāṇaṃ, okkanti nāmarūpaṃ, pasādo āyatanaṃ, phuṭṭho phasso, vedayitaṃ vedanā, ime pañca dhammā idhūpapattibhavasmiṃ purekatassa kammassa paccayā"ti (paṭi. 1.47).
rows.english: according as it is said: “Here [in the present becoming] there is rebirth-linking, which is consciousness; there is descent [into the womb], which is mentality-materiality; there is sensitivity, which is sense base; there is what is touched, which is contact; there is what is felt, which is feeling; thus these five things here in the [present] rebirth-process becoming have their conditions44 in kamma done in the past” (Paṭis I 52).

rows.pali: Tattha paṭisandhi viññāṇanti yaṃ bhavantarapaṭisandhānavasena uppannattā paṭisandhīti vuccati, taṃ viññāṇaṃ.
rows.english: 295. Herein, there is rebirth-linking, which is consciousness means that it is what is called “rebirth-linking” because it arises linking the next becoming that is consciousness.

rows.pali: Okkanti nāmarūpanti yā gabbhe rūpārūpadhammānaṃ okkanti āgantvā pavisanaṃ viya, idaṃ nāmarūpaṃ.
rows.english: There is descent [into the womb], which is mentality-materiality means that it is what consists in the descent of the material and immaterial states into a womb, their arrival and entry as it were, that is mentality-materiality.

rows.pali: Pasādo āyatananti idaṃ cakkhādipañcāyatanavasena vuttaṃ.
rows.english: There is sensitivity, which is sense base: this is said of the five bases beginning with the eye.

rows.pali: Phuṭṭho phassoti yo ārammaṇaṃ phuṭṭho phusanto uppanno, ayaṃ phasso.
rows.english: There is what is touched, which is contact means that it is what is arisen when an object is touched or in the touching of it, that is contact.

rows.pali: Vedayitaṃ vedanāti yaṃ paṭisandhiviññāṇena vā saḷāyatanapaccayena vā phassena saha uppannaṃ vipākavedayitaṃ, sā vedanāti evamattho veditabbo.
rows.english: There is what is felt, which is feeling means that it is what is felt as results [of kamma] that is arisen together with rebirth-linking consciousness, or with the contact that has the sixfold base as its condition, that is feeling. Thus should the meaning be understood.

rows.pali: Idāni hetavo pañcāti taṇhādayo pāḷiyaṃ āgatā taṇhupādānabhavā.
rows.english: 296. (c) There are five causes now as well (§291) means craving, and so on. Craving, clinging and becoming are given in the text.

rows.pali: Bhave pana gahite tassa pubbabhāgā taṃsampayuttā vā saṅkhārā gahitāva honti.
rows.english: But when becoming is included, the formations that precede it or that are associated with it are included too.

rows.pali: Taṇhupādānaggahaṇena ca taṃsampayuttā, yāya vā mūḷho kammaṃ karoti, sā avijjā gahitāva hotīti.
rows.english: And by including craving and clinging, the ignorance associated with them, deluded by which a man performs kamma, is included too.

rows.pali: Evaṃ pañca.
rows.english: So they are five.

rows.pali: Tenāha "idha paripakkattā āyatanānaṃ moho avijjā, āyūhanā saṅkhārā, nikanti taṇhā, upagamanaṃ upādānaṃ, cetanā bhavoti ime pañca dhammā idha kammabhavasmiṃ āyatiṃ paṭisandhiyā paccayā"ti (paṭi. ma. 1.47).
rows.english: Hence it is said: “Here [in the present becoming], with the maturing of the bases there is delusion, which is ignorance; there is accumulation, which is formations; there is attachment, which is craving; there is embracing, which is clinging; there is volition, which is becoming; thus these five things here in the [present] kamma-process becoming are conditions for rebirth-linking in the future” (Paṭis I 52).

rows.pali: Tattha idha paripakkattā āyatanānanti paripakkāyatanassa kammakaraṇakāle sammoho dassito.
rows.english: Herein, the words Here [in the present becoming], with the maturing of the bases point out the delusion existing at the time of the performance of the kamma in one whose bases have matured.

rows.pali: Sesaṃ uttānatthameva.
rows.english: The rest is clear.

rows.pali: Āyatiṃ phalapañcakanti viññāṇādīni pañca.
rows.english: 297.(d) And in the future fivefold fruit: the five beginning with consciousness.

rows.pali: Tāni jātiggahaṇena vuttāni.
rows.english: These are expressed by the term “birth.”

rows.pali: Jarāmaraṇaṃ pana tesaṃyeva jarāmaraṇaṃ.
rows.english: But “ageing-and-death” is the ageing and the death of these [five] themselves.

rows.pali: Tenāha – "āyatiṃ paṭisandhi viññāṇaṃ, okkanti nāmarūpaṃ, pasādo āyatanaṃ, phuṭṭho phasso, vedayitaṃ vedanā, ime pañca dhammā āyatiṃ upapattibhavasmiṃ idha katassa kammassa paccayā"ti (paṭi. ma. 1.47).
rows.english: Hence it is said: “In the future there is rebirth-linking, which is consciousness; there is descent [into the womb], which is mentality-materiality; there is sensitivity, which is sense base; there is what is touched, which is contact; there is what is felt, which is feeling; thus these five things in the future rebirth-process becoming have their condition in kamma done here [in the present becoming]” (Paṭis I 52).

rows.pali: Evamidaṃ vīsati ākārāraṃ hoti.
rows.english: So this [Wheel of Becoming] has twenty spokes with these qualities.

rows.pali: Tivaṭṭamanavaṭṭhitaṃ bhamatīti ettha pana saṅkhārabhavā kammavaṭṭaṃ, avijjātaṇhupādānāni kilesavaṭṭaṃ, viññāṇanāmarūpasaḷāyatanaphassavedanā vipākavaṭṭanti imehi tīhi vaṭṭehi tivaṭṭamidaṃ bhavacakkaṃ yāva kilesavaṭṭaṃ na upacchijjati, tāva anupacchinnapaccayattā anavaṭṭhitaṃ punappunaṃ parivattanato bhamatiyevāti veditabbaṃ.
rows.english: 298.4. With triple round it spins forever (§288): here formations and becoming are the round of kamma. Ignorance, craving and clinging are the round of defilements. Consciousness, mentality-materiality, the sixfold base, contact and feeling are the round of result. So this Wheel of Becoming, having a triple round with these three rounds, should be understood to spin, revolving again and again, forever, for the conditions are not cut off as long as the round of defilements is not cut off.

rows.pali: 655.Tayidamevaṃ bhamamānaṃ,
rows.english: 299. As it spins thus:

rows.pali: Tattha yasmā kusalākusalaṃ kammaṃ avisesena samudayasaccanti saccavibhaṅge vuttaṃ, tasmā avijjāpaccayā saṅkhārāti avijjāya saṅkhārā dutiyasaccappabhavaṃ dutiyasaccaṃ.
rows.english: 300. 1. Herein, [as to source in the truths:] profitable and unprofitable kamma are stated in the Saccavibhaṅga (Vibh 106f.) without distinction as the origin of suffering, and so formations due to ignorance [stated thus] “With ignorance as condition there are formations” are the second truth with the second truth as source.

rows.pali: Saṅkhārehi viññāṇaṃ dutiyasaccappabhavaṃ paṭhamasaccaṃ.
rows.english: Consciousness due to formations is the first truth with the second truth as source.

rows.pali: Viññāṇādīhi nāmarūpādīni vipākavedanāpariyosānāni paṭhamasaccappabhavaṃ paṭhamasaccaṃ.
rows.english: The states beginning with mentality-materiality and ending with resultant feeling, due respectively to consciousness, etc., are the first truth with the first truth as source.

rows.pali: Vedanāya taṇhā paṭhamasaccappabhavaṃ dutiyasaccaṃ.
rows.english: Craving due to feeling is the second truth with the first truth as source.

rows.pali: Taṇhāya upādānaṃ dutiyasaccappabhavaṃ dutiyasaccaṃ.
rows.english: Clinging due to craving is the second truth with the second truth as source.

rows.pali: Upādānato bhavo dutiyasaccappabhavaṃ paṭhamadutiyasaccadvayaṃ.
rows.english: Becoming due to clinging is the first and second truths with the second truth as source.

rows.pali: Bhavato jāti dutiyasaccappabhavaṃ paṭhamasaccaṃ.
rows.english: Birth due to becoming is the first truth with the second truth as source.

rows.pali: Jātiyā jarāmaraṇaṃ paṭhamasaccappabhavaṃ paṭhamasaccanti evaṃ tāvidaṃ saccappabhavato viññātabbaṃ yathārahaṃ.
rows.english: Ageing-and-death due to birth is the first truth with the first truth as source. This, in the first place, is how [the Wheel of Becoming] should be known “as to … source in the four truths” in whichever way is appropriate.

rows.pali: 656.Yasmā panettha avijjā vatthūsu ca satte sammoheti, paccayo ca hoti saṅkhārānaṃ pātubhāvāya.
rows.english: 301. 2. [As to function:] ignorance confuses beings about physical objects [of sense desire] and is a condition for the manifestation of formations;

rows.pali: Tathā saṅkhārā saṅkhatañca abhisaṅkharonti, paccayā ca honti viññāṇassa.
rows.english: likewise [kamma-] formations [582] form the formed and are a condition for consciousness;

rows.pali: Viññāṇampi vatthuñca paṭivijānāti, paccayo ca hoti nāmarūpassa.
rows.english: consciousness recognizes an object and is a condition for mentality-materiality;

rows.pali: Nāmarūpampi aññamaññañca upatthambheti, paccayo ca hoti saḷāyatanassa.
rows.english: mentality-materiality is mutually consolidating and is a condition for the sixfold base;

rows.pali: Saḷāyatanampi savisaye ca pavattati, paccayo ca hoti phassassa.
rows.english: the sixfold base occurs with respect to its own [separate] objective fields and is a condition for contact;

rows.pali: Phassopi ārammaṇañca phusati, paccayo ca hoti vedanāya.
rows.english: contact touches an object and is a condition for feeling;

rows.pali: Vedanāpi ārammaṇarasañca anubhavati, paccayo ca hoti taṇhāya.
rows.english: feeling experiences the stimulus of the object and is a condition for craving;

rows.pali: Taṇhāpi rajjanīye ca dhamme rajjati, paccayo ca hoti upādānassa.
rows.english: craving lusts after lust-arousing things and is a condition for clinging;

rows.pali: Upādānampi upādāniye ca dhamme upādiyati, paccayo ca hoti bhavassa.
rows.english: clinging clings to clinging-arousing things and is a condition for becoming;

rows.pali: Bhavopi nānāgatīsu ca vikkhipati, paccayo ca hoti jātiyā.
rows.english: becoming flings beings into the various kinds of destiny and is a condition for birth;

rows.pali: Jātipi khandhe ca janeti tesaṃ abhinibbattibhāvena pavattattā, paccayo ca hoti jarāmaraṇassa.
rows.english: birth gives birth to the aggregates owing to its occurring as their generation and is a condition for ageing-and-death;

rows.pali: Jarāmaraṇampi khandhānaṃ pākabhedabhāvañca adhitiṭṭhati, paccayo ca hoti bhavantarapātubhāvāya sokādīnaṃ adhiṭṭhānattā.
rows.english: and ageing-and-death ensures the decay and dissolution of the aggregates and is a condition for the manifestation of the next becoming because it ensures sorrow, etc.45

rows.pali: Tasmā sabbapadesu dvedhā pavattikiccatopi idaṃ viññātabbaṃ yathārahaṃ.
rows.english: So this [Wheel of Becoming] should be known accordingly as occurring in two ways “as to function” in whichever way is appropriate to each of its parts.

rows.pali: 657.Yasmā cettha avijjāpaccayā saṅkhārāti idaṃ kārakadassananivāraṇaṃ.
rows.english: 302. 3. [As to prevention:] the clause “With ignorance as condition there are formations” prevents seeing a maker;

rows.pali: Saṅkhārapaccayā viññāṇanti attasaṅkantidassananivāraṇaṃ.
rows.english: the clause “With formations as condition, consciousness” prevents seeing the transmigration of a self;

rows.pali: Viññāṇapaccayā nāmarūpanti "attā"tiparikappitavatthubhedadassanato ghanasaññānivāraṇaṃ.
rows.english: the clause “With consciousness as condition, mentality-materiality” prevents perception of compactness because it shows the analysis of the basis conjectured to be “self”;

rows.pali: Nāmarūpapaccayā saḷāyatanantiādi attā passati - pe - vijānāti, phusati, vedayati, taṇhiyati, upādiyati, bhavati, jāyati, jīyati, mīyatītievamādidassananivāraṇaṃ.
rows.english: and the clauses beginning “With mentality-materiality as condition, the sixfold base” prevent seeing any self that sees, etc., cognizes, touches, feels, craves, clings, becomes, is born, ages and dies.

rows.pali: Tasmā micchādassananivāraṇatopetaṃ bhavacakkaṃ viññātabbaṃ yathārahaṃ.
rows.english: So this Wheel of Becoming should be known “as to prevention” of wrong seeing appropriately in each instance.

rows.pali: 658.Yasmā panettha salakkhaṇasāmaññalakkhaṇavasena dhammānaṃ adassanato andho viya avijjā.
rows.english: 303. 4. [As to similes:] ignorance is like a blind man because there is no seeing states according to their specific and general characteristics;

rows.pali: Andhassa upakkhalanaṃ viya avijjāpaccayā saṅkhārā.
rows.english: formations with ignorance as condition are like the blind man’s stumbling;

rows.pali: Upakkhalitassa patanaṃ viya saṅkhārapaccayā viññāṇaṃ.
rows.english: consciousness with formations as condition is like the stumbler’s falling;

rows.pali: Patitassa gaṇḍapātubhāvo viya viññāṇapaccayā nāmarūpaṃ.
rows.english: mentality-materiality with consciousness as condition is like the appearance of a tumour on the fallen man;

rows.pali: Gaṇḍabhedapīḷakā viya nāmarūpapaccayā saḷāyatanaṃ.
rows.english: the sixfold base with mentality-materiality as condition is like a gathering that makes the tumour burst;

rows.pali: Gaṇḍapīḷakāghaṭṭanaṃ viya saḷāyatanapaccayā phasso.
rows.english: contact with the sixfold base as condition is like hitting the gathering in the tumour;

rows.pali: Ghaṭṭanadukkhaṃ viya phassapaccayā vedanā, dukkhassa paṭikārābhilāso viya vedanāpaccayā taṇhā.
rows.english: feeling with contact as condition is like the pain due to the blow; craving with feeling as condition is like longing for a remedy;

rows.pali: Paṭikārābhilāsena asappāyaggahaṇaṃ viya taṇhāpaccayā upādānaṃ.
rows.english: clinging with craving as condition is like seizing what is unsuitable through longing for a remedy; [583]

rows.pali: Upādiṇṇaasappāyālepanaṃ viya upādānapaccayā bhavo.
rows.english: becoming with clinging as condition is like applying the unsuitable remedy seized;

rows.pali: Asappāyālepanena gaṇḍavikārapātubhāvo viya bhavapaccayā jāti.
rows.english: birth with becoming as condition is like the appearance of a change [for the worse] in the tumour owing to the application of the unsuitable remedy;

rows.pali: Gaṇḍavikārato gaṇḍabhedo viya jātipaccayā jarāmaraṇaṃ.
rows.english: and ageing-and-death with birth as condition is like the bursting of the tumour after the change.

rows.pali: Yasmā vā panettha avijjā appaṭipattimicchāpaṭipattibhāvena satte abhibhavati paṭalaṃ viya akkhīni.
rows.english: Or again, ignorance here as “no theory” and “wrong theory” (see §52) befogs beings as a cataract does the eyes;

rows.pali: Tadabhibhūto ca bālo punabbhavikehi saṅkhārehi attānaṃ veṭheti kosakārakimi viya kosappadesehi.
rows.english: the fool befogged by it involves himself in formations that produce further becoming, as a cocoon-spinning caterpillar does with the strands of the cocoon;

rows.pali: Saṅkhārapariggahitaṃ viññāṇaṃ gatīsu patiṭṭhaṃ labhati pariṇāyakapariggahito viya rājakumāro rajje.
rows.english: consciousness guided by formations establishes itself in the destinies, as a prince guided by a minister establishes himself on a throne;

rows.pali: Upapattinimittaparikappanato viññāṇaṃ paṭisandhiyaṃ anekappakāraṃ nāmarūpaṃ abhinibbatteti māyākāro viya māyaṃ.
rows.english: [death] consciousness conjecturing about the sign of rebirth generates mentality-materiality in its various aspects in rebirth-linking, as a magician does an illusion;

rows.pali: Nāmarūpe patiṭṭhitaṃ saḷāyatanaṃ vuddhiṃ virūḷhiṃ vepullaṃ pāpuṇāti subhūmiyaṃ patiṭṭhito vanappagumbo viya.
rows.english: the sixfold base planted in mentality-materiality reaches growth, increase and fulfilment, as a forest thicket does planted in good soil;

rows.pali: Āyatanaghaṭṭanato phasso jāyati araṇisahitābhimanthanato aggi viya.
rows.english: contact is born from the impingement of the bases, as fire is born from the rubbing together of fire sticks;

rows.pali: Phassena phuṭṭhassa vedanā pātubhavati agginā phuṭṭhassa dāho viya.
rows.english: feeling is manifested in one touched by contact, as burning is in one touched by fire;

rows.pali: Vedayamānassa taṇhā pavaḍḍhati loṇūdakaṃ pivato pipāsā viya.
rows.english: craving increases in one who feels, as thirst does in one who drinks salt water;

rows.pali: Tasito bhavesu abhilāsaṃ karoti pipāsito viya pānīye.
rows.english: one who is parched [with craving] conceives longing for the kinds of becoming, as a thirsty man does for drinks;

rows.pali: Tadassupādānaṃ, upādānena bhavaṃ upādiyati āmisalobhena maccho baḷisaṃ viya.
rows.english: that is his clinging; by clinging he clings to becoming as a fish does to the hook through greed for the bait;

rows.pali: Bhave sati jāti hoti bīje sati aṅkuro viya.
rows.english: when there is becoming there is birth, as when there is a seed there is a shoot;

rows.pali: Jātassa avassaṃ jarāmaraṇaṃ uppannassa rukkhassa patanaṃ viya.
rows.english: and death is certain for one who is born, as falling down is for a tree that has grown up.

rows.pali: Tasmā evaṃ upamāhipetaṃ bhavacakkaṃ viññātabbaṃ yathārahaṃ.
rows.english: So this Wheel of Becoming should be known thus “as to similes” too in whichever way is appropriate.

rows.pali: 659.Yasmā ca bhagavatā atthatopi dhammatopi desanatopi paṭivedhatopi gambhīrabhāvaṃ sandhāya "gambhīro cāyaṃ, ānanda, paṭiccasamuppādo gambhīrāvabhāso cā"ti (dī. ni. 2.95; saṃ. ni. 2.60) vuttaṃ, tasmā gambhīrabhedatopetaṃ bhavacakkaṃ viññātabbaṃ yathārahaṃ.
rows.english: 304. 5. [Kinds of profundity:] Now, the Blessed One’s words, “This dependent origination is profound, Ānanda, and profound it appears” (D II 55), refer to profundity (a) of meaning, (b) of law, (c) of teaching, and (d) of penetration. So this Wheel of Becoming should be known “as to the kinds of profundity” in whichever way is appropriate.

rows.pali: Tattha yasmā na jātito jarāmaraṇaṃ na hoti, na ca jātiṃ vinā aññato hoti, itthañca jātito samudāgacchatīti evaṃ jātipaccayasamudāgataṭṭhassa duravabodhanīyato jarāmaraṇassa jātipaccayasambhūtasamudāgataṭṭho gambhīro.
rows.english: 305. (a) Herein, the meaning of ageing-and-death produced and originated with birth as condition is profound owing to difficulty in understanding its origin with birth as condition thus: Neither does ageing-and death not come about from birth, nor, failing birth, does it come about from something else; it arises [only] from birth with precisely that nature [of ageing-and-death].

rows.pali: Tathā jātiyā bhavapaccaya - pe - saṅkhārānaṃ avijjāpaccayasambhūtasamudāgataṭṭho gambhīro.
rows.english: And the meaning of birth with becoming as condition … and the meaning of formations produced and originated with ignorance as condition are treatable in like manner.

rows.pali: Tasmā idaṃ bhavacakkaṃ atthagambhīranti ayaṃ tāvettha atthagambhīratā.
rows.english: That is why this Wheel of Becoming is profound in meaning. This, firstly, is the profundity of meaning here.

rows.pali: Hetuphalañhi atthoti vuccati.
rows.english: For it is the fruit of a cause that is called “meaning,”

rows.pali: Yathāha – "hetuphale ñāṇaṃ atthapaṭisambhidā"ti (vibha. 720).
rows.english: according as it is said, “Knowledge about the fruit of a cause is the discrimination of meaning” (Vibh 293).

rows.pali: Yasmā pana yenākārena yadavatthā ca avijjā tesaṃ tesaṃ saṅkhārānaṃ paccayo hoti, tassa duravabodhanīyato avijjāya saṅkhārānaṃ paccayaṭṭho gambhīro.
rows.english: 306. (b) The meaning of ignorance as condition for formations is profound since it is difficult to understand in what mode and on what occasion46 ignorance is a condition for the several formations …

rows.pali: Tathā saṅkhārānaṃ - pe - jātiyā jarāmaraṇassa paccayaṭṭho gambhīro, tasmā idaṃ bhavacakkaṃ dhammagambhīranti ayamettha dhammagambhīratā.
rows.english: The meaning of birth as a condition for ageing-and-death is similarly profound. That is why this Wheel of Becoming is profound in law. This is the profundity of law here.

rows.pali: Hetuno hi dhammoti nāmaṃ.
rows.english: For “law” is a name for cause,

rows.pali: Yathāha – "hetumhi ñāṇaṃ dhammapaṭisambhidā"ti (vibha. 720).
rows.english: according as it is said, “Knowledge about cause is discrimination of law” (Vibh 293).

rows.pali: Yasmā cassa tena tena kāraṇena tathā tathā pavattetabbattā desanāpi gambhīrā, na tattha sabbaññutaññāṇato aññaṃ ñāṇaṃ patiṭṭhaṃ labhati.
rows.english: 307. (c) Then the teaching of this [dependent origination] is profound since it needs to be given in various ways for various reasons, and none but omniscient knowledge gets fully established in it;

rows.pali: Tathāhetaṃ katthaci sutte anulomato, katthaci paṭilomato, katthaci anulomapaṭilomato, katthaci vemajjhato paṭṭhāya anulomato vā paṭilomato vā, katthaci tisandhicatusaṅkhepaṃ, katthaci dvisandhitisaṅkhepaṃ, katthaci ekasandhidvisaṅkhepaṃ desitaṃ, tasmā idaṃ bhavacakkaṃ desanāgambhīranti ayaṃ desanāgambhīratā.
rows.english: for in some places in the suttas it is taught in forward order, in some in backward order, in some in forward and backward order, in some in forward or in backward order starting from the middle, in some in four sections and three links, in some in three sections and two links, and in some in two sections and one link. That is why this Wheel of Becoming is profound in teaching. This is the profundity of teaching.

rows.pali: Yasmā cettha yo so avijjādīnaṃ sabhāvo, yena paṭividdhena avijjādayo sammā salakkhaṇato paṭividdhā honti, so duppariyogāhattā gambhīro, tasmā idaṃ bhavacakkaṃ paṭivedhagambhīraṃ.
rows.english: 308. (d) Then the individual essences of ignorance, etc., owing to the penetration of which ignorance, etc., are rightly penetrated as to their specific characteristic, are profound since they are difficult to fathom. That is why this Wheel of Becoming is profound in penetration.

rows.pali: Tathā hettha avijjāya aññāṇādassanasaccāsampaṭivedhaṭṭho gambhīro, saṅkhārānaṃ abhisaṅkharaṇāyūhanasarāgavirāgaṭṭho, viññāṇassa suññataabyāpāraasaṅkantipaṭisandhipātubhāvaṭṭho, nāmarūpassa ekuppādavinibbhogāvinibbhoganamanaruppanaṭṭho, saḷāyatanassa adhipatilokadvārakhettavisayibhāvaṭṭho, phassassa phusanasaṅghaṭṭanasaṅgatisannipātaṭṭho, vedanāya ārammaṇarasānubhavanasukhadukkhamajjhattabhāvanijjīvavedayitaṭṭho.
rows.english: For here the meaning of ignorance as unknowing and unseeing and non-penetration of the truth is profound; so is the meaning of formations as forming and accumulating with and without greed; so is the meaning of consciousness as void, uninterested, and manifestation of rebirth-linking without transmigration; so is the meaning of mentality-materiality as simultaneous arising, as resolved into components or not, and as bending [on to an object] (namana) and being molested (ruppana); so is the meaning of the sixfold base as predominance, world, door, field, and possession of objective field; so is the meaning of contact as touching, impingement, coincidence, and concurrence; so is the meaning of feeling as the experiencing of the stimulus of an object, as pleasure or pain or neutrality, as soulless, and as what is felt;

rows.pali: Taṇhāya abhinanditajjhosānasaritālatānadītaṇhāsamuddaduppūraṭṭho, upādānassa ādānaggahaṇābhinivesaparāmāsaduratikkamaṭṭho, bhavassa āyūhanābhisaṅkharaṇayonigatiṭhitinivāsesukhipanaṭṭho, jātiyā jāti sañjāti okkanti nibbatti pātubhāvaṭṭho, jarāmaraṇassa khayavayabhedavipariṇāmaṭṭho gambhīroti ayamettha paṭivedhagambhīratā.
rows.english: so is the meaning of craving as a delighting in, as a committal to, as a current, as a bindweed, as a river, as the ocean of craving, and as impossible to fill; so is the meaning of clinging as grasping, seizing, misinterpreting, adhering, and hard to get by; so is the meaning of becoming as accumulating, forming, and flinging into the various kinds of generation, destiny, station, and abode; so is the meaning of birth as birth, coming to birth, descent [into the womb], rebirth, and manifestation; and so is the meaning of ageing- and-birth as destruction, fall, break-up and change. This is profundity of penetration.

rows.pali: 660.Yasmā panettha ekattanayo, nānattanayo, abyāpāranayo, evaṃdhammatānayoti cattāro atthanayā honti, tasmā nayabhedatopetaṃ bhavacakkaṃ viññātabbaṃ yathārahaṃ.
rows.english: 309.6. [As to methods:] Then [585] there are four methods of treating the meaning here. They are (a) the method of identity, (b) the method of diversity, (c) the method of uninterest,47 and (d) the method of ineluctable regularity. So this Wheel of Becoming should also be known accordingly “as to the kinds of method. ”48

rows.pali: Tattha avijjāpaccayā saṅkhārā, saṅkhārapaccayā viññāṇanti evaṃ bījassa aṅkurādibhāvena rukkhabhāvappatti viya santānānupacchedo ekattanayo nāma.
rows.english: 310. (a) Herein, the non-interruption of the continuity in this way, “With ignorance as condition there are formations; with formations as condition, consciousness,” just like a seed’s reaching the state of a tree through the state of the shoot, etc., is called the “method of identity.”

rows.pali: Yaṃ sammā passanto hetuphalasambandhena santānassa anupacchedāvabodhato ucchedadiṭṭhiṃ pajahati.
rows.english: One who sees this rightly abandons the annihilation view by understanding the unbrokenness of the continuity that occurs through the linking of cause and fruit.

rows.pali: Micchā passanto hetuphalasambandhena pavattamānassa santānānupacchedassa ekattagahaṇato sassatadiṭṭhiṃ upādiyati.
rows.english: And one who sees it wrongly clings to the eternity view by apprehending identity in the non- interruption of the continuity that occurs through the linking of cause and fruit.

rows.pali: Avijjādīnaṃ pana yathāsakaṃlakkhaṇavavatthānaṃ nānattanayo nāma.
rows.english: 311. (b) The defining of the individual characteristic of ignorance, etc., is called the “method of diversity.”

rows.pali: Yaṃ sammā passanto navanavānaṃ uppādadassanato sassatadiṭṭhiṃ pajahati.
rows.english: One who sees this rightly abandons the eternity view by seeing the arising of each new state.

rows.pali: Micchā passanto ekasantānapatitassa bhinnasantānasseva nānattaggahaṇato ucchedadiṭṭhiṃ upādiyati.
rows.english: And one who sees it wrongly clings to the annihilation view by apprehending individual diversity in the events in a single continuity as though it were a broken continuity.

rows.pali: Avijjāya saṅkhārā mayā uppādetabbā, saṅkhārānaṃ vā viññāṇaṃ amhehīti evamādibyāpārābhāvo abyāpāranayo nāma.
rows.english: 312. (c) The absence of interestedness on the part of ignorance, such as “Formations must be made to occur by me,” or on the part of formations, such as “Consciousness must be made to occur by us,” and so on, is called the “method of uninterestedness.”

rows.pali: Yaṃ sammā passanto kārakassa abhāvāvabodhato attadiṭṭhiṃ pajahati.
rows.english: One who sees this rightly abandons the self view by understanding the absence of a maker.

rows.pali: Micchā passanto yo asatipi byāpāre avijjādīnaṃ sabhāvaniyamasiddho hetubhāvo, tassa aggahaṇato akiriyadiṭṭhiṃ upādiyati.
rows.english: One who sees it wrongly clings to the moral-inefficacy-of-action view, because he does not perceive that the causative function of ignorance, etc., is established as a law by their respective individual essences.

rows.pali: Avijjādīhi pana kāraṇehi saṅkhārādīnaṃyeva sambhavo khīrādīhi dadhiādīnaṃ viya, na aññesanti ayaṃ evaṃdhammatānayo nāma.
rows.english: 313. (d) The production of only formations, etc., respectively and no others with ignorance, etc., as the respective reasons, like that of curd, etc., with milk, etc., as the respective reasons, is called the “method of ineluctable regularity.”

rows.pali: Yaṃ sammā passanto paccayānurūpato phalāvabodhā ahetukadiṭṭhiṃ akiriyadiṭṭhiñca pajahati.
rows.english: One who sees this rightly abandons the no-cause view and the moral-inefficacy- of-action view by understanding how the fruit accords with its condition.

rows.pali: Micchā passanto paccayānurūpaṃ phalappavattiṃ aggahetvā yato kutoci yassa kassaci asambhavaggahaṇato ahetukadiṭṭhiñceva niyatavādañca upādiyatīti evamidaṃ bhavacakkaṃ,
rows.english: One who sees it wrongly by apprehending it as non-production of anything from anything, instead of apprehending the occurrence of the fruit in accordance with its conditions, clings to the no-cause view and to the doctrine of fatalism. So this Wheel of Becoming:

rows.pali: 661.Idañhi atigambhīrato agādhaṃ.
rows.english: 314. There is no one, even in a dream, who has got out of the fearful round of rebirths, which is ever destroying like a thunderbolt, unless he has severed with the knife of knowledge well whetted on the stone of sublime concentration, this Wheel of Becoming, which offers no footing owing to its great profundity and is hard to get by owing to the maze of many methods.

rows.pali: Nānānayagahanato duratiyānaṃ.
Ñāṇāsinā samādhipavarasilāyaṃ sunisitena,
Vuttampi hetaṃ bhagavatā – "gambhīro cāyaṃ, ānanda, paṭiccasamuppādo gambhīrāvabhāso ca.
rows.english: And this has been said by the Blessed One: “This dependent origination is profound, Ānanda, and profound it appears.

rows.pali: Etassa cānanda, dhammassa ananubodhā appaṭivedhā evamayaṃ pajā tantākulakajātā kulāgaṇṭhikajātā muñjapabbajabhūtā apāyaṃ duggatiṃ vinipātaṃ saṃsāraṃ nātivattatī"ti (mahāva. 95; saṃ. ni. 2.60).
rows.english: And, Ānanda, it is through not knowing, through not penetrating it, that this generation has become a tangled skein, a knotted ball of thread, root-matted as a reed bed, and finds no way out of the round of rebirths, with its states of loss, unhappy destinies, … perdition” (D II 55).

rows.pali: Tasmā attano vā paresaṃ vā hitāya ca sukhāya ca paṭipanno avasesakiccāni pahāya,
rows.english: Therefore, practicing for his own and others’ benefit and welfare, and abandoning other duties:
